Its height is modest—often 120–200px—making it feel like a concise summary rather than another dense content block. This placement lets users scan the table, then immediately see a clear, simplified recap of which policy best fits their chosen features and criteria.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the normal vertical stacking in the left column; it does not stick or float and simply pushes any subsequent content downward as needed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy-details-navigator sits in the left column between the feature-highlight-controls and the policy-comparison-table, spanning the full width of the left column and aligned to its grid. It is visually expressed as a compact horizontal bar of tab-like options or segmented controls (for example, 'Overview', 'Coverage Details', 'Exclusions'), with each tab styled as a flat pill or underlined label. The bar is separated from the filters above by roughly 24px and from the table below by another 24px, giving it breathing room but keeping it visually tied to both. Its height is relatively small (around 48–56px), acting like a thin navigation stripe that clearly defines the current comparison mode without overwhelming the content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, the navigator can become sticky within the left column once the user scrolls past its original position, pinning itself just below the global nav so the mode tabs remain visible while scrolling through the lengthy comparison table. It occupies only its natural height and does not overlay content; the table scrolls beneath it. On smaller screens, this stickiness can be disabled and it behaves like a normal in-flow element.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote-start-handler manifests as the primary card in the right sidebar, aligned vertically with the top of the page heading and feature area in the left column. It occupies the full width of the right column (roughly 35–40% of the total page width), with about 24px of horizontal padding between the card and the page edge. The card itself is white, with 20–24px internal padding, soft rounded corners, and a very light shadow, sitting on a slightly warmer gray sidebar background to echo the mockup’s quote card. Inside, a concise heading (e.g., 'Ready to start your boat policy?') sits at the top, followed by one or two lines of microcopy and a large red pill-shaped primary CTA button ('Start a Quote') that runs across most of the card’s width, visually mirroring the strong 'Request a Quote' treatment from the mockup. Additional minor actions (if any) can be vertically stacked beneath the primary button with 16–24px spacing, but the red pill remains the clear focal point.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, the right sidebar anchored by this component behaves as a sticky column: once scrolled slightly, the quote-start card remains fixed within the viewport with a top offset that clears the global navigation (e.g., ~80–96px), so the primary CTA stays visible while the user scrolls the comparison table in the left column. The card’s width remains tied to the right column grid and it does not overlay the left content. On tablet and mobile breakpoints, the sticky behavior is disabled and this component stacks below the main left-column content as a full-width card.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-state-sync component is logically attached around the main comparison experience—conceptually wrapping the feature-highlight-controls, policy-details-navigator, and policy-comparison-table—but it does not render any visual elements. It occupies no explicit space in the two-column grid and does not introduce margins or padding. The user perceives a seamless page, with the URL updating as filters, views, or selected policies change, all without visible UI from this component.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, background behavior attached to the main content container; it has no spatial behavior and does not affect layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component lives in the left column and shares the same horizontal span as the feature-highlight-controls and comparison table. In its inline state, it appears between the policy-details-navigator and the policy-comparison-table, occupying the full width of the left column and presenting either a slim loading strip (with a spinner and short text) or a more prominent alert-style card for errors. The inline card has about 16–20px internal padding and 16–24px vertical margin separating it from the navigator above and the table below, ensuring it stands out without feeling heavy. For full-page loads, it can temporarily overlay the left-column content with a semi-opaque light backdrop and a centered, medium-sized white card (with subtle shadow) containing a loader and message; the right sidebar quote card remains visible, maintaining the two-column balance even during loading.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: When inline, it behaves as a normal block-level element within the left column, pushing the table down. When displaying a blocking full-load state, it positions itself as an overlay within the left column area, absolutely or fixed-positioned relative to that container, covering the table but not the right sidebar, and disappears once loading or error resolution completes.

name: Policy Type Detail
title: Policy Type Detail
description: Displays detailed information for a selected boat insurance policy type, lets the user choose a preferred liability limit, and starts a quote for that policy type using the selected limit.

core_features: Read the policyTypeId from the page URL and load detailed policy type information using getPolicyTypeDetail.
Display the selected policy type’s name, description, relative pricing, and key included benefits.
Show coverage highlights and flag whether towing and uninsured boater coverage are included for this policy type.
Present all allowed liability limit options returned by getPolicyTypeDetail, with one limit selected by default.
Display allowed deductible options for this policy type as informational guidance.
List all optional coverages that are available with this policy type, based on getPolicyTypeDetail.availableOptionalCoverages.
Allow the user to select or confirm a preferred liability limit before starting a quote.
Start a quote for the selected policy type using startQuoteForPolicyType and the chosen liability limit, with entrySource set to policy_type_detail_page.
Navigate the user to quote_boat_details.html with URL parameters for entrySource, policyTypeId, and preferredLiabilityLimit after the quote is started.
Provide navigation back to policy_comparison.html via breadcrumb or a dedicated link.
Handle missing or invalid policyTypeId and API errors by showing clear error states and navigation options.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – View policy details and start a quote:
1) User arrives on policy_type_detail.html with a policyTypeId URL parameter.
2) On page initialization, the page reads policyTypeId from the URL and calls getPolicyTypeDetail(policyTypeId).
3) While the data is loading, a loading state is shown.
4) When getPolicyTypeDetail returns successfully, the page shows the policy name, description, coverage highlights, included benefits, allowed liability limits, allowed deductibles, and available optional coverages.
5) The liability limit selector automatically highlights the defaultLiabilityLimit from the API (if provided); otherwise, the first available option or a predefined selection rule is used without hardcoding specific numeric values.
6) The user can review coverage details, change the liability limit selection, and review which optional coverages are available for this policy type.
7) When ready, the user clicks the "Get a Quote for This Policy" button.
8) The page validates that a liability limit is selected and calls startQuoteForPolicyType with parameters: policyTypeId (from URL / loaded data), preferredLiabilityLimit (currently selected liability limit), and entrySource set to policy_type_detail_page.
9) If startQuoteForPolicyType returns success, the page navigates to quote_boat_details.html with URL parameters entrySource=policy_type_detail_page, policyTypeId=<current policyTypeId>, and preferredLiabilityLimit=<selected liability limit>. Additional parameters such as quoteNumber may also be appended if supported by the target page.
10) If the API call fails, an error message is shown and the user can retry the action.

Workflow 2 – Navigate back to comparison:
1) User is viewing the policy type detail for a specific policyTypeId.
2) User clicks the breadcrumb link or a "Back to Comparison" action.
3) The page navigates to policy_comparison.html without additional parameters, relying on that page’s own logic to display the comparison view.
4) If the user later returns to policy_type_detail.html from that page, the new navigation again passes a policyTypeId in the URL, triggering the load sequence.

Workflow 3 – Handle missing or invalid policyTypeId:
1) User accesses policy_type_detail.html without a policyTypeId parameter or with an invalid value.
2) On initialization, the page attempts to read policyTypeId from the URL and detects that it is missing or invalid.
3) The page does not call getPolicyTypeDetail and instead shows an error state explaining that the requested policy type could not be found.
4) The error state provides an action to return to policy_comparison.html so the user can re-select a policy type.
5) When the user clicks that link, the page navigates to policy_comparison.html.

Workflow 4 – Change liability limit before quoting:
1) User arrives on the policy type detail page and the default liability limit is pre-selected using data from getPolicyTypeDetail.
2) The user reviews the list of allowed liability limits and selects a different option.
3) The selectedLiabilityLimit value in page state is updated to the chosen option.
4) The "Get a Quote for This Policy" button uses the updated selectedLiabilityLimit when calling startQuoteForPolicyType.
5) The subsequent navigation to quote_boat_details.html includes preferredLiabilityLimit set to the newly selected liability limit.
interactions: On page load (or when the policyTypeId URL parameter changes), the page triggers a data load interaction that calls getPolicyTypeDetail with the current policyTypeId. The response populates all policy detail components and initializes selection state such as the default liability limit.

When the user clicks the breadcrumb or "Back to Comparison" link, a navigation interaction occurs that changes the browser location to policy_comparison.html without making additional API calls from this page.

When the user selects a liability limit option (e.g., by clicking a radio button or similar control), an onChange interaction updates the internal page state variable selectedLiabilityLimit to the chosen option’s value. This state is used both for displaying the currently selected limit and for subsequent actions.

When the user clicks the "Get a Quote for This Policy" button, an onClick interaction first validates that a selectedLiabilityLimit is present, then calls startQuoteForPolicyType with the arguments policyTypeId (from state), preferredLiabilityLimit (from selectedLiabilityLimit), and entrySource set to policy_type_detail_page. Based on the API response, the interaction either navigates to quote_boat_details.html with the appropriate URL parameters or shows an error message.

If the getPolicyTypeDetail call fails (e.g., network or server error), an error-handling interaction displays an error message component and provides a retry action that re-invokes getPolicyTypeDetail with the same policyTypeId.

If startQuoteForPolicyType returns a non-success response, an error-handling interaction displays the message returned by the interface (if provided) and offers a retry option without losing the user’s liability limit selection.

If the user uses the browser back button to return to this page from quote_boat_details.html, the page re-runs its initialization logic: it reads policyTypeId from the URL again, re-calls getPolicyTypeDetail, and resets selection state based on the fresh API response, ensuring the UI is consistent with the current URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary state for this page is driven by the policyTypeId URL parameter and data returned from the getPolicyTypeDetail interface. On initialization, the page reads policyTypeId from the query string (for example, policy_type_detail.html?policyTypeId=<value>). If policyTypeId is missing or empty, the page enters an error state and prompts the user to navigate back to policy_comparison.html. If a valid policyTypeId is found, the page calls getPolicyTypeDetail(policyTypeId) and stores the response object in page state (e.g., currentPolicyTypeDetail). The allowedLiabilityLimits and allowedDeductibles arrays, as well as flags like includesOnWaterTowing and includesUninsuredBoaterCoverage, are all derived from this response and are not hardcoded. The page maintains a transient state variable selectedLiabilityLimit that is initialized from defaultLiabilityLimit (if provided by the API) or from another non-hardcoded rule applied to allowedLiabilityLimits. Any user changes to the liability limit update selectedLiabilityLimit in this transient state but do not modify the URL until the user proceeds to quote. When the user clicks the quote CTA and startQuoteForPolicyType succeeds, the page uses window navigation to move to quote_boat_details.html, constructing a URL with query parameters entrySource=policy_type_detail_page, policyTypeId=<current policyTypeId>, and preferredLiabilityLimit=<selectedLiabilityLimit>. The Quote itself is created or updated by startQuoteForPolicyType on the backend; this page does not store the Quote in localStorage and relies only on API responses and URL parameters. Error states (for missing policyTypeId, API failures, or unsuccessful quote starts) are represented in in-memory page state and cleared whenever the page is reloaded or the URL changes.

components.functionality: Reads the policyTypeId from the URL query string, calls getPolicyTypeDetail with that ID, and manages loading, success, and error states. Stores the returned policy type detail object in shared page state for other components to use.

Displays a simple breadcrumb path and provides a clickable link to navigate back to policy_comparison.html. Can optionally show the current policy type name from loaded data as part of the breadcrumb.

Shows the loaded policy type’s name, description, and relative price position using the data from getPolicyTypeDetail. Also indicates whether On-Water Towing and Uninsured Boater Coverage are included for this policy type, based on includesOnWaterTowing and includesUninsuredBoaterCoverage.
Renders the coverageHighlights array returned by getPolicyTypeDetail, displaying each highlight’s title and description to explain major inclusions and benefits of the policy type.

Displays all allowed deductible options for the policy type using the allowedDeductibles array from getPolicyTypeDetail. This component is informational and does not capture a selection on this page; any actual deductible selection can be done on later steps in the quote flow.

Shows all allowed liability limits from getPolicyTypeDetail.allowedLiabilityLimits and lets the user select exactly one. Initializes the selected limit using defaultLiabilityLimit from the API when available. Maintains a selectedLiabilityLimit value in page state so the quote CTA can pass it into startQuoteForPolicyType and into the navigation URL.

Lists the optional coverages that are available for this policy type using the availableOptionalCoverages array returned by getPolicyTypeDetail. Each item shows the coverage name and description so users understand what add-ons may be configurable later in the quote journey.

Displays user-friendly error messages when policyTypeId is missing from the URL or when getPolicyTypeDetail fails. Offers an action to navigate back to policy_comparison.html or to retry loading the data depending on the error state.

Provides the primary "Get a Quote for This Policy" action. When clicked, validates that a liability limit is selected, calls startQuoteForPolicyType with policyTypeId, preferredLiabilityLimit, and entrySource=policy_type_detail_page, then navigates to quote_boat_details.html with URL parameters entrySource, policyTypeId, and preferredLiabilityLimit if the call is successful. Shows API error feedback if the call fails.
Shows a loading state while getPolicyTypeDetail is in progress and hides when the call completes, whether successfully or with an error.

layout.grid_system: Likely a 12-column fixed-width grid (approx. 1140–1200px) centered within the viewport, with the left 7–8 columns for main content and right 4–5 columns for the sidebar CTA/agent column.
layout.layout_pattern: All content sits inside a centered, fixed-width container (~1140–1200px) beneath the global top navigation. At the top of this container, the breadcrumb spans the full content width. Directly below it, any error message occupies a full-width band. Under that, the content splits into a two-column layout: the left column (about 60–65% width) holds the main policy detail narrative and configuration controls in a top-to-bottom stack (summary header, coverage highlights, liability limit selector, deductible options, optional coverages). The right column (about 35–40% width) starts at the same vertical level as the left hero/header and contains the primary quote CTA card. Loading states overlay this entire content area rather than occupying permanent space in the flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All content sits inside a centered, fixed-width container (~1140–1200px) beneath the global top navigation. At the top of this container, the breadcrumb spans the full content width. Directly below it, any error message occupies a full-width band. Under that, the content splits into a two-column layout: the left column (about 60–65% width) holds the main policy detail narrative and configuration controls in a top-to-bottom stack (summary header, coverage highlights, liability limit selector, deductible options, optional coverages). The right column (about 35–40% width) starts at the same vertical level as the left hero/header and contains the primary quote CTA card. Loading states overlay this entire content area rather than occupying permanent space in the flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main page container is centered with generous side gutters. Vertically, the hero zone (breadcrumb + any error + top of two columns including the summary header and quote CTA card) occupies roughly the top 35–40% of the initial viewport. Within the two-column area, the left column uses around 60–65% of the width for text and selectors, while the right column uses 35–40% for the quote card. Section blocks in the left column (lists, selectors) are separated by approximately 24–32px of vertical space; cards and form panels have internal padding around 20–24px. The right quote card has sufficient padding and vertical spacing so it reads as a distinct, prominent card on a light gray page background.
layout_strategies.content_density: The density is medium-low: each component has clear breathing room with 24–32px between major sections and card-like groupings so nothing feels cramped. Text blocks are kept reasonably short, with line lengths controlled by the column width. Interactive elements like the liability limit selector and the quote CTA use generous hit areas and pill-shaped controls consistent with the design DNA. Lists (coverage highlights, optional coverages) are broken into scannable items with consistent vertical rhythm, maintaining a functional, information-forward feel without clutter.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye first moves from the global top navigation down to the breadcrumb at the top left, then to the policy-type summary header in the left column. From that headline, attention is pulled horizontally to the right by the prominent red primary CTA button inside the quote card. From there, the user scans down the left column through the coverage highlights, then to the liability limit selector and deductibles (key decision points), and finally to optional coverages. In parallel, on scroll, the right quote card remains visually dominant and aligned with the user’s choices by reflecting the selected liability limit, reinforcing an F-shaped pattern: across the top (nav and breadcrumb), across the hero (summary header to quote card), and then down the left details with the right CTA card as an anchor.

layout.overall_description: Beneath the global navigation bar, the page content sits in a centered, fixed-width container with a light gray page background. At the very top of this container, a slim, left-aligned breadcrumb rows across the width, giving context without overwhelming the layout. If any error is present, a full-width alert-style bar appears immediately under the breadcrumb, pushing the rest of the content downward but staying visually contained. Below this, the layout splits into two columns: a wider left column for policy details and configuration, and a narrower right column for the quote action. The left column begins with a bold, red-accented policy title and short summary (the hero text), set with comfortable spacing above and below. To its right, starting at the same vertical position, a white, pill-CTA-focused quote card sits on the light gray background, taking up roughly a third of the total width. This card feels like the primary action hub, with a large red pill-shaped 'Get a Quote' button and supporting text about the selected policy and liability limit. As the user scrolls down, the left column continues in a clean vertical stack: key coverage highlights, the liability limit selector, deductible options, and optional coverages, each separated by consistent vertical gaps so the eye can rest. Interactive elements use pill-shaped controls and flat design with subtle shadows, echoing the marketing mockup. On the right, the quote card remains visible for much of the scroll (via a sticky behavior on desktop), updating to reflect the user’s selected limit. A loading indicator, when active, overlays the central area, dimming the background slightly and centering a red-accented spinner or message, while the underlying two-column structure remains intact. The overall effect is a modern, trustworthy, and clearly structured policy detail page that moves users smoothly from understanding coverage to configuring key choices and starting a quote.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader acts as an invisible wrapper that encompasses the entire policy detail content area—from just below the global navigation down to the bottom of the left and right columns. It does not occupy visible space itself; instead, it controls whether the two-column layout, error message, and loading indicator are shown. All visible components (breadcrumb, header, lists, selectors, CTA card) are conceptually children of this loader’s region, which spans 100% of the centered content width while respecting the overall page gutters.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual controller; occupies the full content region logically but does not render any visible UI. Governs whether a centered overlay loading-indicator covers this region or whether the underlying two-column layout is displayed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb appears at the top of the main content container, directly below the global navigation, aligned to the left edge of the left column but allowed to span the full content width. It sits as a single horizontal line of text with subtle red or gray accents, with approximately 24px of top padding from the nav and 16–24px of space below it. It functions as a slim, unobtrusive strip that anchors the page context without competing with the main header or the right CTA card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element positioned at the top of the content flow; scrolls normally with the page and does not overlap other components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy type summary header is the hero text block of the left column. It sits immediately below the breadcrumb (or error message, if present), with about 24–32px of vertical spacing separating it from whatever is above. It is left-aligned, occupying the full width of the left column (roughly 60–65% of the content width), and includes a large, bold heading in a dark neutral or brand red, with a concise supporting subtitle beneath it. The top of this header is horizontally aligned with the top edge of the right-column quote CTA card, creating a unified hero row and reinforcing the F-shaped scan from left headline to right CTA. There is approximately 16–24px of space below the header before the next left-column section begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column’s flow; scrolls naturally while maintaining its initial alignment with the top of the right quote card on load.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coverage highlights list appears directly beneath the summary header in the left column, separated by about 24px of vertical space. It spans the full width of the left column and is presented either as a set of concise bullet points or a simple list of short rows, each on its own line, with 8–12px of spacing between items. The list may be contained within a subtle white or very light card treatment (with roughly 20–24px inner padding) so it feels grouped but not heavy. It remains comfortably away from the left and right edges of the page thanks to the column and card padding, maintaining the medium-low content density. There is 24–32px of space below this list before the next interactive configuration component begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block in the left column’s vertical stack; its height grows with content but it does not affect the alignment of the right column beyond normal flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The deductible options display sits in the left column below the liability limit selector (and above optional coverages), with approximately 24–32px of vertical spacing both above and below to keep it distinct. It spans the full width of the left column and is presented as a horizontal or grid-style arrangement of pill-shaped options or segmented buttons (for example, different deductible amounts), aligned to the left but not touching the column edges due to internal card padding (around 20–24px). If shown inside a card, the card uses a white background with very subtle shadow or border, matching the design DNA. The individual options have generous clickable areas—roughly 12–16px vertical padding and 24–32px horizontal padding—to adhere to the pill CTA aesthetic while keeping the density comfortable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column’s linear flow; expands vertically as needed to accommodate one or two rows of options without affecting the right column’s fixed width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The liability limit selector is placed prominently in the left column as the first major interactive control after the informational coverage highlights. It sits approximately 24–32px below the coverage highlights list and about 24–32px above the deductible options display, effectively forming the center of a 'configuration' band in the left column. It spans the full width of the left column and is presented as a clearly defined form block—likely a white card with 20–24px internal padding, a concise label at the top, and pill-shaped selection controls (buttons, chips, or a slider) beneath. The selector’s controls are horizontally aligned where possible and sized for easy clicking, reflecting the pill CTA styling used elsewhere. This block’s horizontal position aligns visually with the right-column quote card so that users understand that choosing a liability limit on the left directly influences the quote action on the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column; its state is reflected in the right-column quote CTA card, but spatially it remains in place as the user scrolls. On smaller viewports, it stacks naturally above the quote CTA card in a single column layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The optional coverages list appears near the bottom of the left column, below the deductible options display, separated from it by about 24–32px of vertical space. It spans the full width of the left column and presents optional add-ons in a clean, scannable list format—each coverage in its own row with consistent vertical spacing (around 12–16px between items). If the content is more involved, it may be wrapped in a light card with 20–24px padding, but it remains visually lighter than the headline and primary selectors to avoid distracting from the main decision points. This section can extend beyond the fold, with the right-column quote card still visible or quickly reachable, reinforcing that these options are secondary choices after the main liability limit is set.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the lower part of the left column; its height adjusts to the number of optional coverages, potentially driving the overall page height.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error message displays as a full-width alert band within the centered content area, positioned directly below the breadcrumb and above the two-column layout. It stretches across both the left and right column areas (100% of the main content width) and uses a white or lightly tinted background with a red accent border or icon to match the brand while signaling an issue. The band has about 16–20px of internal vertical padding and 20–24px horizontal padding, with 16–24px of vertical space both above (from the breadcrumb) and below (before the summary header and quote card) so it is clearly separated but not overwhelming. When present, it simply pushes the rest of the layout down; when dismissed or absent, the hero header and quote card move up to occupy the space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, inline alert; scrolls with the page and does not overlay content. Appears and disappears above the main hero content without changing column widths.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'Get a Quote for this Policy' CTA component occupies the entire right column and serves as the primary sidebar card. It is aligned so that its top edge matches the top baseline of the summary header in the left column, creating a balanced hero row. The card has a white background on the light gray page, with a subtle shadow or border and generous internal padding (approximately 20–24px on all sides). Inside, the content is vertically stacked: a short, direct heading at the top, a brief line summarizing the currently selected liability limit, followed by a large, red, pill-shaped primary button labelled along the lines of 'Get a Quote' spanning most of the card’s width. Below the main CTA, any supportive text or small secondary link (e.g., details about what happens next) is set with extra spacing so the primary button remains the focal point. The card occupies roughly 35–40% of the total content width and maintains 24–32px of vertical separation from anything above or below it (though typically it is the first item in the right column). On more content-heavy pages, it may appear to float alongside the left column details, staying visually prominent as the user scrolls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a sticky sidebar card on desktop: once the user scrolls past the hero area, the card remains pinned near the top of the viewport within the bounds of the main content container, ensuring the CTA stays visible. On smaller viewports, it falls back to a standard block that stacks below the summary header in a single-column layout and scrolls normally.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading indicator appears over the main content area whenever data is being fetched. It is centered horizontally and vertically within the visible portion of the content container (beneath the global nav), often presenting as a circular spinner or progress glyph in brand red accompanied by a short status label. The rest of the page content either remains hidden or is visually dimmed behind a semi-transparent overlay that covers both the left and right columns, preserving the layout but de-emphasizing it. The indicator itself sits in a compact card or inline block with ample surrounding whitespace (at least 48–64px clear on all sides) so attention is drawn to the loading state without clutter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily displayed as a centered overlay within the bounds of the data-loader’s region. It can be implemented as a fixed or absolute element relative to the content container so that it stays centered while the underlying page is non-interactive during loading.

name: Discounts Overview
title: Discounts Overview
description: Displays current boat insurance discounts with eligibility details and lets users start a new quote with selected discounts pre-applied.

core_features: Fetch and display an overview of all available discounts using the getDiscountsOverview interface.
Show discount name, short description, eligibility information, and maximum savings information (all from API data).
Provide a "Learn More" action on each discount that navigates to the discount_detail.html page with the relevant discountCode URL parameter.
Allow users to select one or more discounts they believe they qualify for, to carry those selections into a new quote.
Provide an "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button that initializes a new quote with the selected discounts via the startNewQuote interface.
On successful quote initialization, navigate the user to quote_boat_details.html with quote and discount context passed via URL parameters.
Display explanatory content (from getDiscountsOverview.introTitle and introBody) to help users understand how discounts may affect their premium.
user_workflows:
View available discounts:
1. User navigates to discounts.html from the header or footer navigation.
2. On page load, the discounts-data-loader component calls getDiscountsOverview with no parameters.
3. While the API call is in progress, the page displays a loading state and disables interactions that depend on discount data.
4. Once data is returned, the discounts-header component displays introTitle and introBody.
5. The discounts-list component renders each discount from the discounts array, including name, shortDescription, eligibilityDescription, and maxSavingsPercent (when present).

Open detailed information for a specific discount:
1. Within the discounts-list component, the user clicks the "Learn More" action on a specific discount row.
2. The click handler reads the discountCode of that row from the API data.
3. The handler navigates the browser to discount_detail.html with a URL parameter discountCode set to that code (e.g., discount_detail.html?discountCode={discountCodeFromAPI}).
4. The discount_detail.html page (not defined here) uses that parameter to load and show full details for the selected discount.

Select discounts in preparation for a quote:
1. In the discounts-list component, each discount row includes a checkbox (or similar selection control) bound to that discount's discountCode.
2. The user toggles one or more discount selection controls.
3. The component maintains an internal list of selected discount codes (selectedDiscountCodes state) based solely on user selection and available API data.
4. The discount-selection-summary component listens to changes in selectedDiscountCodes and updates its summary (such as count of selected discounts) and any contextual messages.
5. The discounts-quote-cta component enables the primary "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button only when at least one discount is selected and the discount data has been successfully loaded.

Start a new quote with selected discounts applied:
1. The user reviews selected discounts in the discount-selection-summary component.
2. The user clicks the "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button in the discounts-quote-cta component.
3. The click handler collects the current list of selected discount codes (selectedDiscountCodes) from the shared page state.
4. The discounts-quote-cta component calls startNewQuote with:
   - entrySource set to "discounts_page".
   - policyTypeId set to null or omitted (unless chosen elsewhere on the page; this page does not require a policy type selection).
   - preselectedDiscountCodes set to the selectedDiscountCodes list.
5. While the API call is in progress, the component displays a loading state and temporarily disables the button to prevent duplicate submissions.
6. If startNewQuote returns success = true, the component reads quoteNumber, step, and appliedDiscountCodes from the response.
7. The component then redirects the browser to quote_boat_details.html, constructing a URL that includes:
   - entrySource=discounts_page
   - quoteNumber={value from response}
   - discountCodes={comma-separated list derived from appliedDiscountCodes in the response}
8. The quote_boat_details.html page (defined elsewhere) uses these URL parameters and the active quote context to continue the quote process with discounts in mind.
9. If startNewQuote returns success = false, the component displays the message from the API response and keeps the user on discounts.html for correction or retry.

Understand how discounts affect premium before starting a quote:
1. After getDiscountsOverview succeeds, the discounts-header and discounts-premium-education-panel components display introTitle and introBody content, which can include explanatory text about how discounts may impact the estimated premium.
2. The discounts-selection-summary component may display additional contextual messages that respond to user selections (for example, indicating that selected discounts will be applied during premium calculation), without assuming any static savings values not provided by the API.
3. If the user proceeds to start a quote, they carry the selected discounts forward, seeing the impact reflected later in the quote flow (outside the scope of this page).
interactions:
Page load and data fetch:
- Event: window.onload / component initialization.
- Handler: discounts-data-loader.onInit.
- Behavior: Calls getDiscountsOverview. On success, updates shared state with introTitle, introBody, and the discounts array; clears any previous error state. On error, sets an error flag and message; dependent components reflect this by showing an error state and disabling quote-related actions.

Selecting or deselecting a discount:
- UI Control: Checkbox or toggle in each discount row.
- Event: onChange on the selection control.
- Handler: discounts-list.onDiscountSelectionChange.
- Behavior: Adds or removes the corresponding discountCode to/from the selectedDiscountCodes list in page state. Triggers re-render of discount-selection-summary and updates the enabled/disabled state of the "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button.

Opening discount details:
- UI Control: "Learn More" link or button within each discount row.
- Event: onClick on the link/button.
- Handler: discounts-list.onLearnMoreClick.
- Behavior: Reads the discountCode of the selected discount from the API-derived data and navigates to discount_detail.html?discountCode={discountCode}.

Starting a quote with selected discounts:
- UI Control: Primary button labeled along the lines of "Apply Discounts to a Quote".
- Event: onClick on the button.
- Handler: discounts-quote-cta.onStartQuoteClick.
- Behavior: Validates that at least one discount is selected and that discount data has been loaded without error. Calls startNewQuote with entrySource="discounts_page" and preselectedDiscountCodes equal to the current selectedDiscountCodes list. Handles loading and error states. On success, redirects to quote_boat_details.html with URL parameters entrySource, quoteNumber, and discountCodes derived from the successful response.

Handling API errors for discounts overview:
- Trigger: getDiscountsOverview returns an error or fails.
- Handler: discounts-data-loader.onError.
- Behavior: Stores an error flag and message. The discounts-header and discounts-list components display a user-friendly error message and hide or disable the main interactive elements relating to discount selection and starting a quote. The "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button remains disabled until the overview can be successfully reloaded (e.g., via a retry interaction, if implemented).

Handling API errors when starting a quote:
- Trigger: startNewQuote returns success = false or fails.
- Handler: discounts-quote-cta.onStartQuoteError.
- Behavior: Displays the message returned from the API, keeps the user on the Discounts Overview page, and re-enables the "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button so the user can adjust selections or retry.
page_functionality.state_logic:
URL parameters and page state:

- Incoming parameters for discounts.html:
  - This page does not require any incoming URL parameters to function. It calls getDiscountsOverview on load and derives all discount-related content from that response.
  - Optionally, the implementation may support an incoming URL parameter selectedDiscountCodes to pre-select discounts when the page loads:
    - Parameter name: selectedDiscountCodes
    - Format: comma-separated list of discount codes (e.g., selectedDiscountCodes=codeA,codeB), where the codes must match discountCode values returned by getDiscountsOverview.
    - Behavior: After getDiscountsOverview succeeds, the discounts-data-loader or discounts-list component compares each discount's discountCode to the values in selectedDiscountCodes. Any matches are added to the initial selectedDiscountCodes state and shown as selected in the UI. Codes that do not match any returned discount are ignored.

- Outgoing navigation parameters from discounts.html:
  - To discount_detail.html:
    - Trigger: User clicks "Learn More" on a discount row.
    - Target URL: discount_detail.html
    - URL parameters:
      - discountCode={discountCode of the selected discount from getDiscountsOverview.discounts}
    - State impact: The user leaves discounts.html and navigates to the detail page. No additional state is stored by this page outside of the navigation URL.

  - To quote_boat_details.html:
    - Trigger: User clicks the "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button and startNewQuote returns success = true.
    - Target URL: quote_boat_details.html
    - URL parameters:
      - entrySource=discounts_page (constant string indicating origin of quote entry).
      - quoteNumber={value returned from startNewQuote.quoteNumber}.
      - discountCodes={comma-separated list derived from startNewQuote.appliedDiscountCodes}. If appliedDiscountCodes is empty or not present, this parameter may be omitted or left empty, according to implementation rules.
    - State impact: The startNewQuote interface sets the active quote for the user on the server side. The quote_boat_details.html page uses quoteNumber and entrySource to load and display details of this new quote and can use the discountCodes parameter for display or validation.

- Internal page state (not stored in URL, but derived from APIs and user actions):
  - discountOverviewState:
    - loading: boolean indicating whether getDiscountsOverview is in progress.
    - error: nullable object or string holding any error message from getDiscountsOverview.
    - introTitle: string from getDiscountsOverview.introTitle.
    - introBody: string from getDiscountsOverview.introBody.
    - discounts: array from getDiscountsOverview.discounts.
  - selectedDiscountCodes:
    - An array of discountCode values representing the current set of discounts selected by the user. Initialized empty or from the selectedDiscountCodes URL parameter once getDiscountsOverview completes.
    - Updated on every selection change in the discounts-list component.
  - quoteInitState:
    - starting: boolean indicating whether startNewQuote is in progress.
    - error: nullable object or string holding any error message from startNewQuote.

- No use of localStorage or other client-side persistence:
  - All persistent state related to quotes is managed via the startNewQuote interface and subsequent pages using URL parameters and server-side data.
  - The Discounts Overview page relies solely on the API responses and temporary in-memory UI state for discount selection.

components.functionality: Initializes the page by calling getDiscountsOverview, manages loading and error states, and provides intro and discount data to other components.
event_handlers: onInit: Triggered when discounts.html is loaded. Calls getDiscountsOverview with no parameters. Sets discountOverviewState.loading=true before the call and sets loading=false after completion.

onSuccess: Invoked when getDiscountsOverview returns successfully. Updates discountOverviewState.introTitle, introBody, and discounts with the response. Clears any previous error state. If the URL includes a selectedDiscountCodes parameter, parses it and initializes selectedDiscountCodes with any codes that match those returned in discounts.

onError: Invoked when getDiscountsOverview fails. Sets discountOverviewState.error with a user-readable error message and discountOverviewState.loading=false. Child components use this information to display error messages and disable interactions that depend on discount data.

components.functionality: Displays the page heading and introductory explanation of discounts, using text returned from getDiscountsOverview.
event_handlers: onStateChange: Listens for changes in discountOverviewState from discounts-data-loader. When introTitle or introBody changes, re-renders the heading and introductory text.
onErrorDisplay: If discountOverviewState.error is set, displays an inline error message instead of or in addition to the intro text.

components.functionality: Renders the list of available discounts with basic information from getDiscountsOverview and allows users to select discounts and open detailed views.
event_handlers: onStateChange: Subscribes to discountOverviewState.discounts from discounts-data-loader. When updated, renders each discount row using fields such as discountCode, name, shortDescription, eligibilityDescription, and maxSavingsPercent (if provided).

onDiscountSelectionChange: Triggered when a user toggles the selection control for a specific discount. Updates the shared selectedDiscountCodes state by adding or removing the corresponding discountCode. Notifies discount-selection-summary and discounts-quote-cta components of the change.

onLearnMoreClick: Triggered when a user clicks the "Learn More" action for a discount. Reads the discountCode associated with that row and navigates the browser to discount_detail.html?discountCode={discountCode}.
onDisableInteractions: If discountOverviewState.loading is true or discountOverviewState.error is set, disables selection controls and "Learn More" actions until a successful data load occurs.

components.functionality: Displays a summary of the user’s currently selected discounts and any contextual guidance based on those selections.
event_handlers: onSelectionChange: Listens to changes in selectedDiscountCodes. Updates the displayed count of selected discounts and can list their names by matching discountCodes with discountOverviewState.discounts.
onOverviewStateChange: When discountOverviewState.error or loading changes, updates the summary display (e.g., hiding content during load or showing a message if discounts cannot be loaded).

components.functionality: Provides educational content about how discounts may influence premiums, based on introBody or other descriptive fields from getDiscountsOverview.
event_handlers: onStateChange: Listens for introBody changes in discountOverviewState and renders explanatory text about discounts and premium calculation.

onSelectionContextUpdate: Optionally listens to selectedDiscountCodes to adjust messaging (for example, indicating that selected discounts will be considered when the premium is calculated), without inferring any specific savings values not provided by the API.

components.functionality: Allows the user to start a new quote with their selected discounts by calling startNewQuote, then navigates to the quote flow with the appropriate URL parameters.
event_handlers: onSelectionChange: Subscribes to selectedDiscountCodes to determine whether the "Apply Discounts to a Quote" button should be enabled. The button is enabled when discountOverviewState.loading is false, discountOverviewState.error is not set, and selectedDiscountCodes has at least one entry.

onStartQuoteClick: When the primary button is clicked, validates the current state (ensuring at least one discount is selected). If valid, sets quoteInitState.starting=true and calls startNewQuote with:
          - entrySource="discounts_page".
          - policyTypeId omitted or null (unless separately selected elsewhere on the page).
          - preselectedDiscountCodes set to selectedDiscountCodes.

onStartQuoteSuccess: Invoked when startNewQuote returns success = true. Updates quoteInitState.starting=false and clears any quoteInitState.error. Reads quoteNumber, step, and appliedDiscountCodes from the response and navigates to quote_boat_details.html with URL parameters entrySource=discounts_page, quoteNumber={quoteNumber}, and discountCodes set to a comma-separated list derived from appliedDiscountCodes.

onStartQuoteError: Invoked when startNewQuote returns success = false or the request fails. Sets quoteInitState.starting=false and quoteInitState.error to the returned or derived error message. Displays this error inline near the CTA and keeps the user on discounts.html.

onOverviewStateChange: If discountOverviewState.loading is true or discountOverviewState.error is set, disables the CTA button and prevents startNewQuote from being called until the overview is successfully loaded.

layout.grid_system: Likely a 12-column fixed-width grid (approx. 1140–1200px) centered within the viewport, with the left 7–8 columns for main content and right 4–5 columns for the sidebar CTA/agent column.
layout.layout_pattern: Below the full-width site navigation and logo, the page uses a two-column layout within a centered, fixed-width container (approx. 1140–1200px). The left column houses the main narrative: the discounts header at the top, followed immediately by the discounts list as the primary content, and then the premium education panel as supporting information. The right column acts as a functional sidebar: the primary quote initiation form sits at the top, aligned horizontally with the left-column header, and the selection summary sits directly beneath it as a supporting card. The data-loader appears at the very top of the content area (or over the list area) when needed, then disappears once data is ready. Content flows linearly top-to-bottom within each column, mirroring the mockup’s structure.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Below the full-width site navigation and logo, the page uses a two-column layout within a centered, fixed-width container (approx. 1140–1200px). The left column houses the main narrative: the discounts header at the top, followed immediately by the discounts list as the primary content, and then the premium education panel as supporting information. The right column acts as a functional sidebar: the primary quote initiation form sits at the top, aligned horizontally with the left-column header, and the selection summary sits directly beneath it as a supporting card. The data-loader appears at the very top of the content area (or over the list area) when needed, then disappears once data is ready. Content flows linearly top-to-bottom within each column, mirroring the mockup’s structure.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: A 12-column grid is implied: the left content column spans roughly 7–8 columns (~60–65% of the container width), and the right sidebar spans 4–5 columns (~35–40%). The main content container is centered in the viewport with generous side gutters. Vertically, the top 'hero band' (header on the left and quote CTA card on the right) occupies roughly the top 35–45% of the initial viewport. Below that, the left column’s discounts list occupies most of the remaining vertical space, while the right column continues with the selection summary card. Spacing between stacked components within a column follows the 8px scale, typically 24–32px gaps between major blocks and 16–24px padding inside cards.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is medium-low. Each major component (header, list, info panel, sidebar cards) has clear breathing room with consistent vertical separation, avoiding a cramped feel. The discounts list is information-heavy but expressed through clearly separated items with sufficient internal padding. The sidebar quote form and summary card are compact but not crowded, prioritizing legibility and ease of interaction. There is enough white space to support scanning and comprehension without appearing sparse.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows an F-shaped pattern. The eye moves left-to-right across the top navigation and brand, then down to the bold red discounts header in the left column. From that headline, attention is drawn across to the right sidebar’s quote CTA card with its strong red primary button. The user then scans downward through the discounts list on the left, occasionally glancing right to the selection summary to understand the impact of their choices. Finally, the eye reaches the premium education panel at the bottom of the left column for deeper understanding. Throughout, strong red accents on headings and CTAs anchor the key interaction points, guiding users from awareness (header) to consideration (list and info) to action (quote form).

layout.overall_description: Beneath a full-width navigation bar, the Discounts Overview page presents a clean, modern two-column layout centered on the screen. The left two-thirds of the content area opens with a prominent red headline explaining boat insurance discounts, followed by a concise supporting subtext. Directly to its right, in a narrower sidebar, a white card with subtle shadow and rounded corners presents the primary 'Start Your Quote' interaction with a large red pill-shaped button and minimal form fields. As the user scrolls down, the left column becomes a vertical stream of clearly separated discount items, each in a lightly padded card-like row, while the right column continues with a smaller summary card that reflects the user’s selected discounts and potential savings. Further down the left side, an information panel explains how discounts affect premiums, functioning like an educational section that wraps up the page narrative. The overall effect is a functional, corporate marketing page: spacious but information-rich, with red accents and strong CTAs that naturally pull the eye from the top-left headline to the right-side quote form and then down through the discounts details.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader is positioned at the very top of the main content area, directly below the global navigation bar and above both left and right columns. Visually, it appears as a slim, full-width horizontal loading element spanning 100% of the content container, aligned with the combined width of the two-column grid. When active, it occupies a small vertical height (approximately 4–8px for a progress bar or up to 48px if represented as a status strip), with space above and below kept minimal so it feels attached to the nav rather than a primary content block. If implemented as skeleton loaders, these would appear within the left column in the approximate footprint of the discounts list, maintaining the same column width and alignment, while the right sidebar quote card stays in place. Once loading completes, the loader disappears entirely, allowing the discounts-header and discounts-quote-cta to sit at the top of their respective columns with no gap.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily displayed and then removed; positioned in the normal document flow at the top of the content container or overlaying the discounts list area. It is not sticky or fixed; it scrolls with the page if present long enough, but is expected to disappear quickly after data is loaded.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The discounts header anchors the top of the left column, starting approximately 48–64px below the global navigation bar, aligning vertically with the top edge of the right-column quote CTA card. It spans the full width of the left column (about 60–65% of the container), with the main headline set in a large, bold red type that immediately catches the eye. A short subtitle or explanatory line sits directly beneath the headline with 8–16px spacing, using a smaller, neutral color. The header block has generous top and bottom padding (roughly 16–24px internal spacing) but no enclosing card border, maintaining a clean, flat look against the page background. Beneath the header, there is a clear vertical gap of about 24–32px before the first item of the discounts list, ensuring the header reads as a distinct hero-like introduction to the content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard, top-aligned within the left column and part of the normal scrollable flow. It scrolls out of view as the user moves down the page; it is not sticky.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The discounts list sits immediately below the discounts header in the left column, separated by approximately 24–32px of vertical space. It occupies the full width of the left column and extends downward, forming the main content stream users scroll through. Each discount appears as a distinct row or card-like block with 16–24px of internal padding and clear separation (16–24px) between adjacent items, echoing the spacious yet information-focused cards in the mockup. If a grid is used (for example, discount chips or icons), items are arranged in one or two sub-columns within the left column, but the overall list never encroaches into the sidebar area. The list’s vertical extent is the largest single block on the page, filling most of the screen height below the hero band and continuing beyond the fold. At the bottom of the list, there is a clear 32–40px gap before the premium education panel, signaling the transition from interactive selection to explanatory content. Throughout, alignment remains flush with the left column edges, keeping a clean, linear reading path.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows naturally in the left column as standard scrollable content. No fixed or sticky behavior; items simply stack vertically as the user moves down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The selection summary is placed in the right sidebar, directly below the discounts-quote-cta card, with a vertical gap of about 24–32px between them. It spans the full width of the right column (approximately 35–40% of the content container) and is styled as a compact white card with subtle shadow and softly rounded corners to match the quote card. Internal padding is around 16–20px on all sides, giving room for a clear, concise summary of the discounts selected and indicative savings. The card’s top edge aligns horizontally with the start of its content, not the quote card’s internal elements, preserving a simple stacked-card appearance. Vertically, this summary card sits high enough that at least the top portion remains visible above the fold on most screens, so users can see the impact of their selections without excessive scrolling. As the discounts list on the left grows taller, the selection summary scrolls down in parallel, maintaining a right-hand context panel that feels clearly related to the user’s choices.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Positioned in the normal flow of the right sidebar, stacked below the quote CTA card. It scrolls with the page and is not fixed, though its position near the top of the sidebar keeps it visible early in the scroll experience.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The premium education panel is located in the left column beneath the discounts list, separated from the last discount item by approximately 32–40px of vertical space. It spans the full width of the left column and is presented as a single informational card or block, with 20–24px of padding on all sides. The panel features a medium-weight subheading (potentially in a muted red or dark neutral) at the top, followed by explanatory paragraphs that clarify how discounts influence the overall boat insurance premium. The card’s visual treatment is subtle—white background, light border or very soft shadow—ensuring it feels important but clearly secondary to the interactive discounts list. This panel typically occupies about one-third to half of the visible viewport height, depending on content length, and serves as a natural conclusion to the left-column narrative. A generous bottom margin (32–48px) separates it from the end of the main content, preventing the page from feeling abruptly cut off.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the normal scrollable flow at the bottom of the left column. It has no sticky or fixed behavior and simply appears as the user scrolls past the bulk of the discounts list.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote initiation form is the primary card in the right sidebar and is aligned with the top of the discounts header in the left column, forming the right side of the hero band. It sits approximately 48–64px below the global navigation, matching the top margin of the left column. The card spans the full width of the right column (about 35–40% of the content container), with generous internal padding of 24px on all sides. The card has a white background, subtle drop shadow, and rounded corners, clearly distinguishing it from the gray or neutral page background. At the top of the card, a short, strong headline in dark or red text introduces the action (e.g., 'Start Your Boat Insurance Quote'), followed by a small amount of explanatory text and minimal form fields (e.g., ZIP code, boat type) arranged in a vertical stack with 12–16px spacing. The primary action is a large red pill-shaped button that either spans most of the card’s width or is centered horizontally with ample side padding, positioned near the bottom of the card with extra spacing above and below to maintain clarity. This card occupies roughly the top 35–45% of the sidebar’s height above the fold on typical screens, making the CTA highly visible as soon as the page loads.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Top-aligned within the right sidebar as part of the normal scrollable flow. It is not sticky, but due to its initial position and height, it remains on-screen for the first portion of the user’s scroll, emphasizing the primary conversion path.

name: Discount Detail
title: Discount Detail
description: Displays detailed information for a single discount based on the discountCode URL parameter, explains eligibility and how to apply it, and lets the user start a new boat insurance quote with this discount context.

core_features: Read the discountCode URL parameter and fetch full discount details via getDiscountDetail.
Display discount name, short description, eligibility criteria, required documentation, and maximum potential savings using dynamic data from getDiscountDetail.
Explain how the discount is applied in the quote process using the howToApply field from getDiscountDetail.
Provide navigation back to the Discounts overview page without altering discount state.
Initialize a new quote with this discount preselected via startNewQuote and then navigate the user to the quote boat details step.
Handle loading, missing discountCode, and error states when retrieving discount details or starting a quote.
user_workflows: View discount details from Discounts page: 1) User clicks a discount on discounts.html, which navigates to discount_detail.html with a discountCode URL parameter. 2) Discount Detail page reads discountCode from the URL. 3) Page calls getDiscountDetail(discountCode). 4) On success, page shows the discount information and eligibility details. 5) User may read how to qualify and what documentation is needed.

Start a quote with this discount: 1) On the Discount Detail page, user clicks the primary action to start a quote with this discount. 2) Page calls startNewQuote with entrySource set to the appropriate value for the discounts flow and preselectedDiscountCodes containing the current discountCode from the URL. 3) If startNewQuote returns success, page redirects the user to quote_boat_details.html with URL parameters indicating the entry source and discountCodes derived from the current discountCode. 4) The quote wizard continues using the discount context provided by the initialized quote.

Return to Discounts overview: 1) User clicks the breadcrumb or a "Back to Discounts" control. 2) Page performs a navigation to discounts.html with no additional parameters. 3) No calls are made to startNewQuote when simply navigating back.

Handle missing or invalid discountCode: 1) User arrives at discount_detail.html without a discountCode or with a value that results in an error from getDiscountDetail. 2) Page displays an error state indicating that the discount cannot be loaded. 3) User can use a provided control to navigate back to discounts.html to select a valid discount. 4) Optionally, user may retry loading by triggering a reload of getDiscountDetail for the current discountCode if applicable.
interactions: On initial load, the page reads the discountCode value from the URL query string and triggers a data fetch via getDiscountDetail; no user action is required for this interaction.
Clicking the breadcrumb or "Back to Discounts" interaction triggers client-side navigation to discounts.html, discarding the current discount-specific view but not modifying any quote data.

Clicking the primary "Start a Quote with This Discount" action triggers a call to startNewQuote using entrySource, an optional policyTypeId if available from context (or left empty), and preselectedDiscountCodes containing the current discountCode; on success, a redirect to quote_boat_details.html is performed with relevant URL parameters.

If getDiscountDetail fails (for example, due to a network error or nonexistent discountCode), the user can click a retry control that re-invokes getDiscountDetail with the same discountCode from the URL.

If startNewQuote returns a failure (success is false), the user sees an inline error message sourced from the message field and can click the quote-start button again to retry after the issue is resolved.

If the user or browser modifies the discountCode query parameter and the page is reloaded with a different discountCode, the page re-runs getDiscountDetail with the new parameter and refreshes all bound discount details accordingly.
page_functionality.state_logic: The main state driver is the discountCode URL parameter. On page initialization, the application parses discountCode from the query string of discount_detail.html. If discountCode is present, the page enters a loading state and calls getDiscountDetail(discountCode). While awaiting the response, the UI shows a loading indicator and disables quote-start actions. If getDiscountDetail resolves successfully, the page transitions to a 'loaded' state in which the discount detail components are populated with the returned name, shortDescription, eligibilityDescription, maxSavingsPercent, documentationRequired, and howToApply values. If the request fails (for example, an error response or network error), the page sets an 'error:discountDetailLoad' state and renders an error message with options to retry or navigate back to discounts.html. If no discountCode is present in the URL at all, the page immediately enters a 'missingDiscountCode' state and shows a message directing the user back to discounts.html. When the user clicks the start-quote action from a valid loaded state, the page sets a 'startingQuote' state and calls startNewQuote with entrySource set to the designated value representing the discounts entry, policyTypeId left unset or derived from any existing context if available, and preselectedDiscountCodes populated with the current discountCode from the URL. If startNewQuote returns success true, the page triggers a navigation to quote_boat_details.html including entrySource and discountCodes in the query string so that downstream pages can recognize the discounts-origin flow and applied discount codes. If startNewQuote returns success false or the call fails, the state changes to 'error:quoteStart' and displays the returned message, while keeping the discount detail content visible so the user can retry. Any subsequent change to the discountCode in the URL (for example, by navigating to a new discount_detail.html?discountCode=...) causes the initialization logic to run again, resetting state and fetching details for the new discount.

components.functionality: Parses the discountCode from the discount_detail.html query string and exposes it to other components. Triggers the initial call to getDiscountDetail when a valid discountCode is present and manages transitions between 'missingDiscountCode', 'loading', 'loaded', and 'error:discountDetailLoad' states.

Shows or hides a generic loading state while discount details are being fetched or while a new quote is being initialized, based on page state set by the URL state manager and the quote-start component.

Displays error messages when the discountCode is missing, when getDiscountDetail fails, or when startNewQuote fails. Provides contextual actions to retry loading details or navigate back to the Discounts overview.
Displays a simple breadcrumb path that includes a link back to the Discounts overview and the current discount name when available.
Shows the discount name and shortDescription retrieved from getDiscountDetail. This component is only rendered when discount details have successfully loaded.
Displays eligibility criteria text using the eligibilityDescription field from getDiscountDetail. May include structured bullets if the text returned by the interface provides separators.
Shows the documentationRequired information returned by getDiscountDetail so the user knows what proof or documents may be needed for this discount.
Summarizes potential savings for the discount using maxSavingsPercent returned from getDiscountDetail, explaining that actual savings are determined dynamically during the quote.
Explains how the user should apply this discount during the quote process using the howToApply field returned by getDiscountDetail, including any steps that will occur on later quote pages.

Provides the main action to start a new quote with the current discount context. On click, calls startNewQuote with entrySource set to the appropriate value for discounts-origin quotes, policyTypeId left empty or passed from context if available, and preselectedDiscountCodes set to the discountCode from the URL. Handles success by redirecting to quote_boat_details.html with entrySource and discountCodes URL parameters, and handles failure by exposing an error message.
Dedicated control that navigates back to discounts.html when activated, providing an alternative to the breadcrumb for returning to the discounts list.

layout.grid_system: Likely a 12-column fixed-width grid (approx. 1140–1200px) centered within the viewport, with the left 7–8 columns for main content and right 4–5 columns for the sidebar CTA/agent column.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a two-column marketing layout beneath the global header: a dominant left column for the discount’s descriptive content and a narrower right sidebar for the primary quote CTA. Content in each column flows linearly from top to bottom. Shared elements like the breadcrumb and any error message sit above the two-column split, spanning the overall content width but visually aligned with the left column. Supporting discount sections (eligibility, savings, how-to-apply, documentation) stack vertically in the left column, while the right column houses a single, framed action panel that remains visually present as the user scrolls through the details.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a two-column marketing layout beneath the global header: a dominant left column for the discount’s descriptive content and a narrower right sidebar for the primary quote CTA. Content in each column flows linearly from top to bottom. Shared elements like the breadcrumb and any error message sit above the two-column split, spanning the overall content width but visually aligned with the left column. Supporting discount sections (eligibility, savings, how-to-apply, documentation) stack vertically in the left column, while the right column houses a single, framed action panel that remains visually present as the user scrolls through the details.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The layout follows roughly a 12-column grid with the left column taking about 7–8 columns (~60–65% width) and the right sidebar 4–5 columns (~35–40%), separated by a generous gutter. Vertically, the header plus sidebar action panel form the top ‘hero band’ of the page. Below that, the left column expands downward with multiple information sections, while the right sidebar maintains its single card-like action panel with consistent top and bottom margins relative to the grid. Sections and cards use 24–32px vertical gaps, and all cards/panels use 20–24px internal padding.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is medium-low: headings, cards, and sections have clear breathing room, with generous white space around the main discount header and the right CTA card. Text blocks in each information section are kept compact and legible, with 16–24px spacing between headings and paragraphs. The savings summary is slightly more visually emphasized but still clean and uncluttered. Overall, the page feels informative yet airy, avoiding crowded blocks of text.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow follows an F-shaped pattern adapted from the mockup: the eye moves from the global navigation to the breadcrumb, then down to the large discount header on the left. From there, attention is pulled across to the bold red primary CTA inside the right sidebar action panel. The user then scans downward through the left column: savings summary first, then eligibility, how-to-apply, and documentation, occasionally bouncing their gaze back to the persistent right-hand quote panel. Any error or loading states temporarily override this flow by appearing prominently near the top or centered, respectively.

layout.overall_description: All content is centered within a fixed-width container (approximately 1140–1200px) on a light gray page background. Just below the global site header, a subtle breadcrumb row runs across the top of the content area, aligned with the left side of the main column. Under this, the layout splits into a two-column grid: the left two-thirds host a bold, red-accented discount title and short description, while the right one-third begins with a clean white card that asks the user to start a quote with this discount and features a large red pill-shaped CTA button. As the user scrolls, the left column continues with a lightly separated savings highlight card, then stacked information sections for eligibility, how to apply the discount, and any related documentation. Each section is separated by generous vertical spacing and clear typographic hierarchy. The right sidebar contains only the single action panel, vertically centered relative to its column’s content, maintaining a sense of focus and stability rather than changing cards. At the very bottom of the left column, a simple text link leads back to the full discounts list. The result is a focused, corporate marketing layout where the discount information is easy to scan, and the primary next step—starting a quote—remains continuously visible and visually dominant in the right column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is a non-visual, infrastructural component with no direct on-screen footprint. Conceptually, it sits at the root of the Discount Detail page layout, controlling whether the loading indicator, error message, and main two-column content are shown. It does not occupy any space in the grid; instead, it orchestrates state and data for all other components that render within the centered 12-column container.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Purely logical; no rendered box or positioning in the layout, but governs the presence/absence of other components in the normal document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When active, the loading indicator appears as an overlay centered within the main content area, covering both the left content column and the right sidebar. It sits within the same overall fixed-width container but uses a semi-opaque white or light-gray scrim that dims the underlying layout. A spinner or simple loading icon is centered horizontally and vertically within the visible portion of the container, occupying roughly a small card-sized area (around 20–30% of the container width) with ample padding and subtle shadow. The breadcrumb and underlying columns remain in place but are visually de-emphasized beneath the overlay.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Positioned absolutely or fixed relative to the main content container, covering the two-column grid while active; removed entirely when loading completes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error message appears as a full-width system alert bar within the centered content container, directly beneath the global header and breadcrumb and above the two-column layout. It spans nearly the full content width (all 12 grid columns) but remains aligned with the left column’s starting edge, leaving consistent left and right padding to the viewport edges. The error block has 16–24px internal padding, a red-accented border or background, and an icon on the left, with text wrapping to the right. It pushes the header and right sidebar action panel downward, maintaining 24–32px of vertical space between the bottom of the alert and the start of the discount detail header. When visible, it becomes the first element users see after the breadcrumb, clearly separated by a small (16px) gap above and below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in normal document flow, pushing other components down when rendered; no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb navigation is positioned at the top of the Discount Detail content, directly beneath the global site header. It spans the full width of the content container but is visually anchored to the left, starting at the left edge of the main column with a comfortable left margin from the viewport edge. The breadcrumb text is small and subtle, with 8–12px of padding above and below, and about 16–24px of vertical space separating it from the discount header row below (or from any error message if present). It sits above the split into left and right columns, acting as a horizontal preface to the main layout.
