component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard inline/block positioning at the top of the content stack; scrolls off-screen as the user moves down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The discount detail header serves as the hero headline of the page and occupies the upper-left portion of the two-column grid. It spans roughly 7–8 grid columns (about 60–65% of the content width) starting from the left edge of the content container, leaving a generous gutter to its right before the sidebar. The header includes a large, bold title (often in or accented with the brand red), an optional short subtitle, and possibly a small metadata chip (e.g., ‘Boat policy discount’), stacked vertically with 8–16px spacing between them. The top edge of this header aligns horizontally with the top edge of the right-column action panel, creating a unified hero band across the page. Below the header, there is 24–32px of vertical space before the next left-column section (the savings summary).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A standard block-level element within the left column of the grid; scrolls normally with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The eligibility section appears in the left column beneath the savings summary card. It spans the same 7–8 grid columns as the header, aligning to the left column boundaries. The section begins with a clear subheading (e.g., ‘Eligibility’) in a medium-weight type, followed by body text and/or a short bullet list describing who qualifies. The heading and content are stacked vertically with around 12–16px spacing, and there is 24–32px of vertical space above the section (separating it from the savings summary) and 24–32px below (separating it from the how-to-apply section). There is no heavy card framing here; the section sits on the page background with perhaps a subtle bottom divider line, keeping it clean and easy to scan in a linear downward flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows as a standard content block within the left column, expanding vertically as needed based on text length.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The documentation section is placed in the left column below the how-to-apply section, occupying the same width as the other left-column content. It starts with a descriptive subheading (e.g., ‘Documentation & Proof’) aligned with the left edge of the text column, followed by one or more paragraphs or a brief list detailing any documents needed to claim the discount. Internally, headings and text are spaced by 12–16px. A vertical gap of 24–32px separates this section from the how-to-apply section above, and a slightly larger gap of 32–40px may separate it from the bottom ‘Back to discounts’ link below, giving a sense of closure to the informational content. Visually, it has similar weight to the eligibility and how-to-apply sections, maintaining a consistent, lightly structured text presentation.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block element in the left column, stretching vertically as content demands and scrolling off-screen as the user moves down.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The savings summary is a highlighted informational card in the left column, positioned directly beneath the main discount header. It spans the full width of the left column (7–8 grid columns) and is visually framed as a white card on the light gray page background, with subtle corner rounding and a very light shadow or border. Inside the card, there is 20–24px padding on all sides. The content typically includes a compact heading (e.g., ‘Your Savings’) and one or two key lines of text or numeric highlights (e.g., ‘Save up to 15% on qualifying boat policies’), arranged vertically with 8–16px between lines. The top of the card sits about 24px below the bottom of the header, and another 24–32px of space appears below the card before the eligibility section. This card gently draws attention as a summary of value without overpowering the primary CTA in the right sidebar.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a standard card within the left column; no sticky behavior, scrolling naturally with surrounding sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The how-to-apply section is positioned in the left column between the eligibility and documentation sections. It aligns with the same left and right boundaries as the other text sections, occupying 7–8 grid columns. At the top, a clear subheading (e.g., ‘How to Apply This Discount’) is followed by a short step-by-step description or numbered list explaining how the discount will be applied during the quote or policy process. Internal spacing between the heading and steps is around 12–16px, and between individual steps 8–12px. Vertically, it sits with 24–32px of whitespace above and below, maintaining the spacious, medium-density feel. This section is structured to be easily scannable while the right-hand action panel remains visible on the screen.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A normal text block in the left column, participating in the linear scroll flow of the main content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The start-quote action panel is the primary right-sidebar card and visually echoes the quote card from the mockup. It occupies the right 4–5 grid columns (about 35–40% of the content width), starting at the same vertical level as the top of the discount header on the left. The card is set on a light gray page background and presented as a white rectangle with subtle corner rounding and a very light, soft shadow. Inside, 24px of padding surrounds a concise title (e.g., ‘Start a Boat Quote with This Discount’), one short supporting line explaining what happens next, and a prominent red pill-shaped button (‘Start My Quote’) that spans most of the card’s width but leaves comfortable left and right margins. These elements are stacked vertically with 12–20px spacing between them. Below the main button, there may be a small line of secondary text or a subtle link (e.g., ‘Learn more about our policies’) with extra 8–12px spacing. The card remains visually anchored near the top of the right column, with 24–32px of space above it (from the breadcrumb area) and a similar gap below before any page footer, ensuring it feels stable and always available as a primary action while the user reads through the left-column content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A fixed-position card within the right column of the grid in terms of layout; it scrolls naturally with the page (no sticky behavior) but stays visually compact so that it often remains in view alongside the upper and mid-level left-column content on typical screen heights.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The back-to-discounts link is placed at the very bottom of the left column, directly beneath the documentation section. It aligns with the left edge of the text content and spans only as wide as the link text itself (e.g., ‘← Back to all discounts’). There is a slightly larger vertical gap (around 32–40px) between the bottom of the documentation section and this link, creating a clear visual boundary that signals the end of the main discount details. The link styling is understated but clear—typically brand-colored text with a simple left-pointing chevron icon. Below this link, there is enough bottom padding (32–48px) before any global footer elements, preserving the page’s open, modern feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A small inline link element at the bottom of the left column; it scrolls off-screen with the rest of the page and does not affect the right sidebar.

name: Quote  Boat Details
title: Boat Quote – Boat Details
description: First step of the online boat insurance quote process where users provide boat and location details, validate online eligibility, and proceed to coverage options.

core_features: Initialize a new quote based on URL context (entrySource, policyTypeId, discountCodes) using the startNewQuote interface when no quoteId is provided.
Resume an existing quote when a quoteId URL parameter is present by loading current boat details and form configuration via getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig.
Render a dynamic boat details form (boat type, length, value, ZIP, usage, and optional descriptive fields) using configuration and prefilled values from getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig.
Validate boat details for required fields, numeric/value ranges, and eligibility via server-side validation in updateQuoteBoatDetails, with field-level error feedback.
Evaluate online eligibility based on updateQuoteBoatDetails.eligibility and guide the user accordingly if the boat is not eligible for online quoting.
Show high-level premium estimates when available (summaryPremiumEstimateMonthly and summaryPremiumEstimateAnnual) after boat details are submitted successfully.
Advance the user to the coverage options step (quote_coverage_options.html) with the quoteId URL parameter when updateQuoteBoatDetails succeeds and nextStep indicates coverage_options.
Allow users to cancel the quote process at this step and return to the homepage (index.html) via explicit navigation controls.
user_workflows:
New quote from a CTA (no quoteId in URL):
1. User clicks a "Get a Quote" or similar CTA on another page, navigating to quote_boat_details.html with entrySource set, and optionally policyTypeId and discountCodes in the URL.
2. On page load, the quote-init-manager component reads URL parameters.
3. Because quoteId is absent, the component calls startNewQuote with:
   - entrySource: taken from the entrySource URL parameter.
   - policyTypeId: taken from the policyTypeId URL parameter if present, otherwise left undefined.
   - preselectedDiscountCodes: parsed from the discountCodes URL parameter into an array of strings (if present).
4. When startNewQuote returns success, the component stores the returned quoteNumber and any initialBoatDetails and selectedPolicyType in in-memory page state.
5. The component then calls getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig to retrieve boatTypeOptions, usageOptions, value/length constraints, and currentBoatDetails for the active quote context.
6. The boat-details-form component populates all fields using the returned options and currentBoatDetails.
7. The user completes or edits the form fields.
8. The user clicks the "Continue" button in the wizard navigation, which triggers the form submission (updateQuoteBoatDetails).

Resuming an existing quote from account dashboard (quoteId in URL):
1. User clicks a "Resume Quote" action on account_dashboard.html, navigating to quote_boat_details.html with a quoteId URL parameter.
2. On page load, the quote-init-manager detects the presence of quoteId and enters "resume" mode. It does not call startNewQuote.
3. The server or surrounding application uses quoteId to establish the active quote context.
4. The quote-init-manager calls getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig without parameters.
5. getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig returns configuration and currentBoatDetails for the active quote. The form is prefilled with these values.
6. The user reviews and updates boat details as needed.
7. The user clicks "Continue", triggering updateQuoteBoatDetails with the current form values.

Submitting boat details and handling validation/eligibility:
1. User clicks "Continue" in the wizard navigation.
2. The boat-details-form runs any client-side checks (e.g., required fields not empty, numeric inputs parse correctly). If client-side checks fail, inline-validation-messages are displayed and the submission is blocked.
3. If client-side checks pass, the form calls updateQuoteBoatDetails with the current field values: boatType, lengthFeet, boatValue, primaryZip, usage, year, make, model, hullMaterial.
4. If updateQuoteBoatDetails returns success=false and includes errors, the inline-validation-messages component maps each error.field to the associated form control and surfaces error.message inline, keeping the user on this page.
5. If updateQuoteBoatDetails returns eligibility.isEligibleOnline=false, the eligibility-status-panel displays eligibility.reason as a blocking message and disables further progression to coverage options from this step.
6. If updateQuoteBoatDetails returns success=true, eligibility.isEligibleOnline=true, and nextStep indicates coverage_options, the eligibility-status-panel can optionally show summaryPremiumEstimateMonthly and summaryPremiumEstimateAnnual if provided.
7. The quote-progress-navigation then redirects the user to quote_coverage_options.html with a quoteId URL parameter set to the returned quoteNumber.

Navigating away or cancelling the quote:
1. At any time after the form is loaded, the user may click a "Cancel" or "Back to Home" control.
2. The quote-progress-navigation component intercepts this and performs a simple client-side navigation to index.html without modifying quote data.
3. No additional interfaces are called as part of this cancellation action.

Handling page reloads or direct deep links:
1. If a user reloads the page with a quoteId in the URL, the page re-enters the resume workflow, calling only getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig for the active quote context.
2. If a user reloads the page without quoteId but with entrySource and optional policyTypeId/discountCodes, the page re-initializes a quote by calling startNewQuote again, producing a new quoteNumber; subsequent navigation to coverage options uses that new quoteNumber.
interactions:
Page load and initialization:
- onPageLoad: The quote-init-manager reads URL parameters (entrySource, policyTypeId, discountCodes, quoteId) and decides whether to call startNewQuote or only getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig.
- onConfigLoaded: When getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig returns, the boat-details-form populates dropdowns and input defaults, then transitions the UI out of a loading state.

Form field interactions:
- onBoatTypeChange: Updates the selected boat type in local form state; may also clear related validation errors for that field.
- onUsageChange: Updates selected usage; used later by updateQuoteBoatDetails for rating and eligibility.
- onLengthChange / onValueChange: Captures numeric input; client-side validation runs on blur or input to ensure the values are within the minBoatValue/maxBoatValue and maxOnlineBoatLengthFeet thresholds provided by getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig (without hardcoding these constraints).
- onZipChange: Captures primaryZip input; triggers client-side formatting checks and clears any server-side errors related to ZIP when changed.
- onOptionalFieldChange (year, make, model, hullMaterial): Updates these optional descriptive fields in form state and clears any related error messages.

Form submission and validation:
- onSubmit (Continue button): Triggers client-side validation; if valid, calls updateQuoteBoatDetails with current form state.
- onServerValidationResponse: The inline-validation-messages component receives updateQuoteBoatDetails.errors and updates the UI to highlight invalid fields.
- onEligibilityResponse: The eligibility-status-panel receives updateQuoteBoatDetails.eligibility and, if available, premium estimates. It updates its messaging, and informs quote-progress-navigation whether progression to the next step is allowed.

Navigation interactions:
- onContinueNavigation: After a successful updateQuoteBoatDetails call where eligibility.isEligibleOnline is true and nextStep indicates coverage_options, quote-progress-navigation constructs the URL quote_coverage_options.html?quoteId={quoteNumber} and navigates there.
- onCancelClick: Immediately navigates to index.html without calling any interfaces.
- onBackToDashboard (if provided as a simple link): Navigates to account_dashboard.html using a standard link, without altering quote data.

Error and edge-case handling interactions:
- onStartNewQuoteError: If startNewQuote returns success=false, an error banner is shown in the eligibility-status-panel describing message, and the boat-details-form remains disabled or in a non-interactive state until the user reloads or navigates away.
- onConfigError: If getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig fails (e.g., unable to load configuration), an error message is displayed and form controls are disabled.
- onUnexpectedNextStep: If updateQuoteBoatDetails returns a nextStep value other than coverage_options, the eligibility-status-panel can display a generic message instructing the user that the process cannot continue online from this step, and quote-progress-navigation does not navigate away.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page state is driven entirely by URL parameters, in-memory UI state, and responses from the three assigned interfaces.

URL Parameters:
- entrySource: If quoteId is not present, this is read and passed directly into startNewQuote as the entrySource parameter. It also implicitly defines how the quote is labeled in back-end systems but is not otherwise used for branching logic on this page.
- policyTypeId: If provided and quoteId is not present, this is passed into startNewQuote so that the new Quote is associated with a specific PolicyType context. The returned selectedPolicyType may be used for messaging within the page but does not alter the fields collected.
- discountCodes: If provided and quoteId is not present, the raw string is parsed by the page into an array of codes (splitting on commas) and passed to startNewQuote as preselectedDiscountCodes. The resulting appliedDiscountCodes returned by startNewQuote are retained in page state but do not directly affect fields on this page.
- quoteId: When present, the page assumes the user is resuming an existing quote rather than starting a new one. The presence of quoteId causes the initialization logic to skip calling startNewQuote and instead rely on the hosting application or server to treat this quoteId as the active quote context used implicitly by getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig and updateQuoteBoatDetails.

Page Modes:
- New Quote Mode (no quoteId): On load, startNewQuote is called with values from entrySource, policyTypeId, and discountCodes. If successful, the page stores quoteNumber and proceeds to fetch configuration via getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig. All subsequent calls to getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig and updateQuoteBoatDetails operate on this newly created, active quote. When the user continues, navigation to quote_coverage_options.html uses the quoteNumber returned by updateQuoteBoatDetails as the quoteId URL parameter.
- Resume Quote Mode (quoteId present): On load, the page does not call startNewQuote. Instead, it calls getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig, which is assumed to return configuration and currentBoatDetails for the quote referenced by quoteId. The page does not modify the URL; it simply uses quoteId for identification when constructing the next-step URL after successful submission.

In-memory UI State:
- Active Quote Summary: Stores quoteNumber, selectedPolicyType, and appliedDiscountCodes as returned from startNewQuote or derived from existing context. This state is not persisted outside the current page session and is only used for navigation and display.
- Form State: Stores current values of boatType, boatLengthFeet, boatValue, primaryZip, usage, year, make, model, hullMaterial, initialized from currentBoatDetails on first load and updated on each user interaction.
- Validation State: Stores client-side validation flags and mappings from field names to error messages. This is updated after client validation and upon receiving updateQuoteBoatDetails.errors.
- Eligibility State: Stores the latest eligibility object and summary premium estimates from updateQuoteBoatDetails. This state determines whether progression to the next step is allowed and what messages are displayed.

Navigation Rules:
- Proceeding to Coverage Options: Only occurs after a call to updateQuoteBoatDetails where success is true, eligibility.isEligibleOnline is true, and nextStep is equal to the expected coverage options step label. In that case, the page navigates to quote_coverage_options.html?quoteId={quoteNumber}, where quoteNumber is taken from the updateQuoteBoatDetails response.
- Cancelling: Navigates directly to index.html without calling any interface or altering quote state.

The page does not use localStorage or any other client-side persistence. All durable state is handled by the back-end through the Quote and Boat models, and is accessed via the assigned interfaces using only data derived from URL parameters and current in-memory form state.

components.functionality: Controls initial page mode (new quote vs resume), orchestrates calls to startNewQuote and getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig based on URL parameters, and exposes configuration and active quote metadata to other components.
event_handlers: onPageLoad: Reads entrySource, policyTypeId, discountCodes, and quoteId from the URL query string and determines initialization mode.

onNewQuoteInit: When quoteId is absent, calls startNewQuote(entrySource, policyTypeId, preselectedDiscountCodes) and stores quoteNumber, initialBoatDetails, selectedPolicyType, and appliedDiscountCodes on success.
onResumeQuoteInit: When quoteId is present, skips startNewQuote and assumes the server sets the active Quote context from quoteId.
onConfigRequest: After establishing the active quote context, calls getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig to retrieve boatTypeOptions, usageOptions, constraints, and currentBoatDetails.
onConfigResponse: Passes configuration and currentBoatDetails to the boat-details-form and updates loading/error states.

components.functionality: Displays and manages the boat details input fields required for the quote (boat type, length, value, primary ZIP, usage, and optional year/make/model/hullMaterial) using dynamic configuration, and submits these details via updateQuoteBoatDetails.
event_handlers: onConfigBind: Receives boatTypeOptions, usageOptions, maxOnlineBoatLengthFeet, minBoatValue, maxBoatValue, and currentBoatDetails from quote-init-manager and initializes form fields.
onInputChange: Updates internal form state as the user edits text and numeric fields.
onSelectChange: Updates internal form state for dropdowns such as boatType, usage, and hullMaterial.
onFieldBlur: Performs client-side checks (e.g., required fields, numeric parsing, comparing values against provided constraints) and updates validation state.

onSubmit: After client-side validation passes, calls updateQuoteBoatDetails with current form values (boatType, lengthFeet, boatValue, primaryZip, usage, year, make, model, hullMaterial) and forwards the response to inline-validation-messages, eligibility-status-panel, and quote-progress-navigation.

components.functionality: Manages and displays field-level validation errors originating from both client-side checks and updateQuoteBoatDetails.errors, ensuring users see clear guidance per field.
event_handlers: onClientValidationResult: Receives validation results from boat-details-form (e.g., missing required fields or invalid formats) and displays messages next to the relevant inputs.
onServerValidationResult: Receives the errors array from updateQuoteBoatDetails and maps each error.field and error.message to the corresponding input control.
onFieldChangeClear: Clears error messages for specific fields when the user updates those fields.

components.functionality: Presents the outcome of eligibility checks and high-level premium estimates after boat details are submitted, and indicates whether the user can proceed online or must stop at this step.
event_handlers: onEligibilityUpdate: Receives eligibility from updateQuoteBoatDetails and updates the panel state to reflect whether isEligibleOnline is true or false.
onPremiumEstimateUpdate: Receives summaryPremiumEstimateMonthly and summaryPremiumEstimateAnnual from updateQuoteBoatDetails and displays them if provided.
onBlockingIneligibleState: When eligibility.isEligibleOnline is false, shows eligibility.reason and notifies quote-progress-navigation that continuing should be disabled.
onReset: Clears eligibility and premium messages when the user edits key fields that could change eligibility (e.g., boatType, boatLengthFeet, boatValue).

components.functionality: Controls navigation actions for this wizard step, including triggering form submission on "Continue", preventing progression when ineligible, and handling cancellation to the home page.
event_handlers: onContinueClick: Initiates boat-details-form submission. It does not navigate directly but waits for the result of updateQuoteBoatDetails.

onUpdateResult: Receives the updateQuoteBoatDetails response; if success is true, eligibility.isEligibleOnline is true, and nextStep indicates coverage options, constructs quote_coverage_options.html?quoteId={quoteNumber} using quoteNumber and performs navigation.
onIneligibleState: When notified by eligibility-status-panel that eligibility.isEligibleOnline is false, disables the Continue action until eligibility changes.
onCancelClick: Navigates directly to index.html without invoking any interfaces.
onStepDisplay: Shows the current step label (e.g., "Boat Details") and marks it as active in the wizard’s internal state, without affecting any back-end data.

components.functionality: Provides a single source of truth for URL-derived state (entrySource, policyTypeId, discountCodes, quoteId) and exposes this state to other components, ensuring consistent behavior when the URL changes or the page reloads.
event_handlers: onPageLoad: Parses the current URL query string to extract entrySource, policyTypeId, discountCodes, and quoteId, and exposes them as observable values.
onUrlChange: If the URL changes (e.g., via browser navigation), re-parses parameters and notifies quote-init-manager of any changes.
onExposeParams: Supplies parsed parameters to quote-init-manager so it can decide whether to call startNewQuote or only getQuoteBoatDetailsFormConfig.

layout.grid_system: Likely a 12-column fixed-width grid (approx. 1140–1200px) centered within the viewport, with the left 7–8 columns for main content and right 4–5 columns for the sidebar CTA/agent column.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a centered, fixed-width (≈1140–1200px) 12-column grid beneath the global top navigation. Directly under the site nav, a full-width quote-progress-navigation bar spans all 12 columns as a slim horizontal band. Below that, the main content is split into a two-column layout: the left 7–8 columns are reserved for the boat-details-form and its inline-validation-messages, arranged in a strictly top-to-bottom linear flow; the right 4–5 columns form a sidebar containing the eligibility-status-panel, also stacked top-to-bottom. Non-visual utility components (quote-init-manager, url-parameter-inspector) are positioned logically in the DOM but do not significantly affect the visual arrangement.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a centered, fixed-width (≈1140–1200px) 12-column grid beneath the global top navigation. Directly under the site nav, a full-width quote-progress-navigation bar spans all 12 columns as a slim horizontal band. Below that, the main content is split into a two-column layout: the left 7–8 columns are reserved for the boat-details-form and its inline-validation-messages, arranged in a strictly top-to-bottom linear flow; the right 4–5 columns form a sidebar containing the eligibility-status-panel, also stacked top-to-bottom. Non-visual utility components (quote-init-manager, url-parameter-inspector) are positioned logically in the DOM but do not significantly affect the visual arrangement.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Within the main content container, approximately 60–65% of the width (about 7–8 grid columns) is allocated to the left main column for the boat-details-form. The remaining 35–40% (about 4–5 columns) forms the right sidebar for the eligibility-status-panel. Vertically, the quote-progress-navigation occupies a shallow band at the top of the content area (≈64px tall including padding), followed by a generous vertical gap (≈32px) before the two-column section begins. Within each column, components are separated by consistent vertical spacing (≈24–32px between cards/blocks, ≈16–24px between related elements). At the very bottom of the page, the url-parameter-inspector occupies a low-profile strip spanning the full content width, visually separated from the main experience.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is medium-low: the form fields are clearly grouped with comfortable spacing between them, and the eligibility-status-panel has generous internal padding and breathing room. There is enough text and state feedback (validation and eligibility messages) to feel functional and informative, but white space is deliberately preserved around each block. Inline-validation-messages are concise and tucked just below inputs with small vertical separation, avoiding visual overload while keeping errors visible.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow follows an F-shaped pattern. The user’s eye moves from the global top nav down to the center-aligned quote-progress-navigation bar. From there, the gaze drops to the left-side boat-details-form heading and first fields. As the user starts scanning down the form, the right-side eligibility-status-panel, aligned to the same vertical start, pulls attention across to the right as a key secondary focal point. Scrolling continues vertically down the form, with occasional side glances to the eligibility-state updates in the right column. Validation messages appear immediately under fields, reinforcing a top-down reading order. The debug url-parameter-inspector sits unobtrusively at the bottom, only entering the visual flow when intentionally expanded.

layout.overall_description: Beneath the site’s red-accented top navigation, the content is centered in a clean, fixed-width container. At the top of this container, a slim, full-width quote-progress-navigation bar runs horizontally, visually anchoring the step-based quote process. Below it, the page splits into a modern two-column layout: on the left, a spacious boat details form begins with a strong title and stacked input groups flowing downward; on the right, a white eligibility-status-panel card sits on a subtle gray background, aligned to the top of the form like the quote card in the mockup. The left column occupies roughly two-thirds of the width, giving the form room for clear labels and comfortable field spacing, while the right column takes the remaining third, presenting the eligibility card as a focused, secondary information hub. Throughout, components sit in pill-shaped and card-like containers with 20–24px internal padding and 24–32px gaps between sections, maintaining a light, trustworthy corporate feel. Inline validation hints appear just beneath inputs, and as users move down the form, the right-hand panel updates in parallel, keeping the eye moving in an F-shaped path. At the bottom of the page, separated by extra spacing and visually de-emphasized, a compact url-parameter-inspector strip provides technical insight without intruding on the primary quoting experience.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote-init-manager is a non-visual, state-handling component positioned logically near the top of the page structure, conceptually just inside the main quote page container and above the visible quote-progress-navigation. It occupies no visible width or height on the screen and does not create spacing in the grid. From a layout perspective, it sits behind the scenes, initializing and managing quote state before any visible form elements render.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendering utility; no spatial footprint. It is placed early in the DOM hierarchy to initialize state for all visual components but does not affect layout flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote-progress-navigation appears directly below the global site header, centered within the fixed-width content container. It spans the full 12-column grid (≈100% of the content container width), forming a horizontal band across the top of the quote page. Vertically, it is relatively shallow—roughly 48–64px tall including padding—but visually prominent through red accents and step indicators. There is comfortable white space above and below it: a smaller margin (≈16–24px) separating it from the site nav and a larger margin (≈32px) beneath it before the two-column main content begins. The navigation elements (steps, labels, and possibly a progress pill or bar) are horizontally distributed, with equal left and right padding to align with the form and sidebar columns below, reinforcing the grid structure.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrolls with the page in the normal document flow. Optionally may become subtly sticky at the top of the content area on taller pages, but by default it sits as a standard block just under the main site navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The boat-details-form occupies the left main column in the two-column layout. It begins just below the quote-progress-navigation, separated by a vertical gap of about 32px. Horizontally, it spans roughly 7–8 of the 12 grid columns, equating to about 60–65% of the content container width. The left edge of the form aligns with the left edge of the progress navigation content, preserving a clean vertical grid line. At the top of the form block, there is a prominent page or section title (e.g., “Boat Details”) styled in the brand’s red, functioning similarly to the hero headline in the mockup. Below the title, form fields are arranged in a single-column, top-to-bottom stack, grouped logically (e.g., boat basics, location, usage). Each input row (label + field) occupies nearly the full width of the left column, with side padding of approximately 24px inside a subtle card or panel, and 16–24px vertical spacing between individual rows. Field groups are separated by slightly larger gaps (≈24–32px) and may include subheadings in muted text. The bottom of the form aligns visually with the bottom of the eligibility-status-panel when possible, though the form may extend further downward as more fields are added, causing the right column to end earlier and leaving pale gray background under it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element in the left column that scrolls normally with the page. On narrower screens, it responsively shifts to full width above the eligibility-status-panel, but on desktop it maintains its 7–8 column width in the two-column grid.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The inline-validation-messages component lives inside the boat-details-form, occupying small regions directly beneath individual form inputs. Each message sits left-aligned under its associated field, starting at the same left edge as the input text, and spans roughly the same width as the input (typically most of the left column width minus internal padding). Vertically, each message is separated from the field above by a small gap of about 4–8px, reflecting the 8px base spacing unit, and from the next field below by the usual 16–24px field spacing. When no validation issues are present, this component either collapses completely, taking up no space, or leaves only a minimal reserved gap that does not visually distract. When active, messages use red text and subtle iconography consistent with the brand, but remain low-profile, ensuring they support the form’s readability rather than overpowering it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline within the form’s normal flow. Messages dynamically expand and collapse within each field group without shifting the surrounding grid columns; they only affect local vertical spacing inside the boat-details-form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The eligibility-status-panel is presented as a prominent, white card in the right sidebar column, visually mirroring the quote/CTA card from the mockup. It begins at the same vertical starting line as the boat-details-form’s title or first fields, creating a strong horizontal alignment across the page. Horizontally, it spans the remaining 4–5 grid columns on the right, approximately 35–40% of the content container width. The card has generous internal padding (around 20–24px on all sides) and rests on a very light gray sidebar background, with a subtle, soft shadow or thin border to separate it from the background. Inside the card, eligibility status information is laid out top-to-bottom: a concise heading (e.g., “Eligibility Status”), a short explanatory sentence or two, then a prominent status indicator (icon plus text, possibly colored green or amber), and any secondary notes below. Total card height above the fold is moderate, mirroring the quote card’s proportion in the mockup, and there is a clear vertical gap of approximately 24–32px beneath it before any additional sidebar content (or simply whitespace). As the form is completed, this panel updates but retains its position, remaining visible alongside the upper and mid portions of the form during normal scrolling.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block element in the right column that scrolls with the page. On large screens, it stays adjacent to the upper and middle parts of the form; on small screens, it stacks below the boat-details-form, spanning full width with the same card styling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-parameter-inspector sits at the very bottom of the quote page’s main content area, spanning the full 12-column width of the fixed content container but styled to be visually subdued. It is separated from the preceding content (typically the lower portion of the boat-details-form) by a larger vertical gap of about 32–48px to clearly distinguish it as a technical, non-customer-facing tool. In its collapsed state, it appears as a slim horizontal bar—roughly 32–40px tall—aligned with the content width, anchored to the bottom of the viewport or just above the page footer, with a small label like “Debug URL Parameters” on the left and a subtle toggle icon on the right. When expanded, it grows vertically to a panel of about 160–240px height, displaying monospaced text or JSON-like content in a scrollable area. The background is a muted gray with a faint border, ensuring it does not compete visually with the primary red-accented content above.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Typically collapsed and optionally sticky to the bottom of the viewport for developer use. When expanded, it overlays the very bottom of the page content but does not alter the main grid structure; instead, it floats above the background while maintaining left/right alignment with the central content container.

name: Quote  Coverage Options
title: Quote Coverage Options
description: Page where users review and adjust coverage options (policy type, liability limits, deductibles, and discount eligibility) for an in-progress boat insurance quote before viewing plan options.

core_features: Load current quote coverage context (selected policy type, available policy types, liability limit options, deductible options, discount eligibility options, and estimated premiums) using getQuoteCoverageOptions based on the quoteId URL parameter.
Allow users to view and change the policy type for the quote (when multiple policy types are available) and immediately update the quote and estimated premiums via updateQuoteCoverageSelections.
Allow users to choose a liability limit from the available liabilityLimitOptions, including any recommended options, and persist the selection via updateQuoteCoverageSelections.
Allow users to choose a deductible from the available deductibleOptions, including any recommended options, and persist the selection via updateQuoteCoverageSelections.
Display discount eligibility options and allow users to mark themselves as eligible or ineligible; apply these changes and refresh estimated premiums using updateQuoteDiscountEligibility.

Continuously display an estimated monthly and annual premium summary, updated after each coverage or discount change using responses from updateQuoteCoverageSelections or updateQuoteDiscountEligibility.

Validate coverage selections and discounts when the user clicks the "View Plans" action by calling updateQuoteCoverageSelections and, on success, navigate to the plans list step (quote_plans.html) with the appropriate URL parameters.
Provide a way to return to the previous boat details step (quote_boat_details.html) while preserving the current quote context via the quoteId/quoteNumber URL parameter.

Surface API validation errors (e.g., invalid combinations of liability limit and deductible) from updateQuoteCoverageSelections and updateQuoteDiscountEligibility so users can correct their selections before proceeding.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Standard progression from boat details to plans list
1. User arrives from quote_boat_details.html with a quoteId in the URL.
2. Page reads quoteId from the URL and calls getQuoteCoverageOptions to load current selections and options for that quote.
3. User reviews the preselected policy type, liability limit, deductible, and estimated premiums displayed on the page.
4. User optionally changes policy type, liability limit, and/or deductible. Each change triggers updateQuoteCoverageSelections, which returns updated estimatedPremiumMonthly and estimatedPremiumAnnual; the page updates the displayed selections and premium summary.
5. User reviews available discount eligibility options and toggles applicable ones. Each toggle triggers updateQuoteDiscountEligibility with the full current discountSelections list; the response updates the appliedDiscountCodes and estimated premiums shown in the premium summary.
6. Once satisfied, the user clicks the primary "View Plans" action. The page calls updateQuoteCoverageSelections with the current liabilityLimit, deductible, policyTypeId, and discountSelections to ensure the quote state is consistent and valid.
7. If updateQuoteCoverageSelections succeeds and returns nextStep indicating the plans list, the page navigates to quote_plans.html with URL parameters quoteId set to the returned quoteNumber and sortBy set to price_asc.
8. User arrives on the plans list page with coverage and discount selections already applied in the quote.

Workflow 2: Returning user from account dashboard adjusting coverage before picking a plan
1. User clicks a "Resume Quote" or similar link from account_dashboard.html, which opens quote_coverage_options.html with a quoteId in the URL.
2. Page reads quoteId and calls getQuoteCoverageOptions to retrieve the latest coverage and discount state for that quote.
3. User sees their previously selected policy type, liability limit, deductible, discount selections, and estimated premium.
4. User updates only one aspect (for example, changes the deductible). The change triggers updateQuoteCoverageSelections, which updates the quote and returns refreshed estimated premiums; the page updates the display.
5. User may also adjust discount eligibility (e.g., marks a newly completed safety course). The page calls updateQuoteDiscountEligibility with updated discountSelections; estimated premiums refresh if discounts are applied.
6. User clicks "View Plans" to proceed, triggering updateQuoteCoverageSelections with the current state; on success, the page navigates to quote_plans.html as in Workflow 1.

Workflow 3: User corrects boat details and returns to coverage options
1. User is on quote_coverage_options.html with an active quote loaded (quoteNumber known from getQuoteCoverageOptions or previous updates).
2. User decides to change boat details and clicks the "Back" link.
3. The page uses the current quoteNumber as quoteId and navigates to quote_boat_details.html?quoteId={quoteNumber}.
4. After modifying boat details, the previous step routes the user back to quote_coverage_options.html with the same quoteId.
5. The page again calls getQuoteCoverageOptions to reload coverage options, which may differ due to updated boat characteristics (as provided by the backend). The user then proceeds as in Workflow 1.
interactions:
Page load with quoteId in URL:
- Trigger: Browser loads quote_coverage_options.html with ?quoteId={value}.
- Behavior: The controller component reads the quoteId from the URL. If present, it calls getQuoteCoverageOptions to fetch coverage options and current selections. If missing or if the API response indicates an error, the page shows an appropriate error state and disables coverage interactions until a valid quote context exists.

Policy type change:
- Trigger: User selects a different policy type option in the policy-type-selector (e.g., via radio buttons or dropdown populated from availablePolicyTypes).
- Behavior: The controller compiles the current liabilityLimit, deductible, chosen policyTypeId, and current discountSelections and calls updateQuoteCoverageSelections. On a successful response, it updates the selectedPolicyType, selectedLiabilityLimit, selectedDeductible (if adjusted by the server), appliedDiscountCodes, and estimatedPremiumMonthly/estimatedPremiumAnnual in state. If errors are returned, they are displayed in an error-summary component and the selection is either reverted or kept but marked invalid, based on design.

Liability limit change:
- Trigger: User selects a different liability limit option from liabilityLimitOptions in the liability-deductible-selector.
- Behavior: The controller calls updateQuoteCoverageSelections with the new liabilityLimit, the current deductible, policyTypeId, and current discountSelections. On success, state and premium summary are updated; on error, a message is shown and the UI may revert to the last valid liability limit.

Deductible change:
- Trigger: User selects a different deductible option from deductibleOptions.
- Behavior: The controller calls updateQuoteCoverageSelections with the new deductible and current liabilityLimit, policyTypeId, and discountSelections. The response updates the quote’s selectedDeductible and estimated premiums. Errors are displayed if the selection is invalid.

Discount toggle:
- Trigger: User checks or unchecks a discount eligibility option (e.g., a checkbox in discount-eligibility-form) representing one item from discountEligibilityOptions.
- Behavior: The discount-eligibility-form updates its local representation of discountSelections (for all discount codes) and calls updateQuoteDiscountEligibility with this updated list. On success, the page updates appliedDiscountCodes and estimatedPremiumMonthly/estimatedPremiumAnnual. If the response indicates failure or includes a message (e.g., eligibility requirements not met), the message is surfaced in the UI and the discount control may show an error state or helper text.

View Plans action:
- Trigger: User clicks the primary "View Plans" or equivalent button in the coverage-options-form.
- Behavior: The form is validated client-side (e.g., ensuring a policy type, liability limit, and deductible are selected) before submission. Then the controller calls updateQuoteCoverageSelections with the current liabilityLimit, deductible, policyTypeId, and discountSelections to ensure the backend has the latest consistent state. If success is true and any returned errors array is empty, and/or nextStep indicates the plans list, the page navigates to quote_plans.html with URL parameters quoteId set to the returned quoteNumber and sortBy set to price_asc. If errors are returned, they are displayed and navigation is halted.

Back to boat details:
- Trigger: User clicks the "Back" link/button.
- Behavior: The controller reads the current quoteNumber from its state (from the last successful getQuoteCoverageOptions or updateQuoteCoverageSelections response) and navigates to quote_boat_details.html?quoteId={quoteNumber}. If quoteNumber is not available, it falls back to the original quoteId from the URL if present, or shows an error preventing navigation.

Error handling and display:
- Trigger: Any call to getQuoteCoverageOptions, updateQuoteCoverageSelections, or updateQuoteDiscountEligibility returns an error or success=false.
- Behavior: The error-summary component displays messages from the errors array or message field. For field-specific errors, the corresponding form fields are marked with error states. A dismiss action on the error-summary can clear visible error messages without altering underlying state.

URL change / direct navigation:
- Trigger: User manually alters the quoteId in the URL or arrives from an external source.
- Behavior: On each page load, the controller uses the current URL quoteId only to scope the initial getQuoteCoverageOptions call. If the quoteId changes (e.g., through a full page reload with a different URL), previous in-memory state is discarded and a new quote context is loaded. Outgoing navigation always uses the server-returned quoteNumber rather than the raw URL quoteId to ensure consistency with backend identifiers.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The primary external state driver is the quoteId URL parameter. On initial load, the page reads quoteId from the query string and uses it implicitly in getQuoteCoverageOptions (the backend associates the request with the correct quote). The response provides quoteNumber, which is treated as the canonical identifier for this quote for subsequent navigation (e.g., when moving to quote_plans.html or back to quote_boat_details.html).

Internal page state (held in the controller component) includes: selectedPolicyType, availablePolicyTypes, liabilityLimitOptions, deductibleOptions, selectedLiabilityLimit, selectedDeductible, discountEligibilityOptions with isSelected flags, appliedDiscountCodes, estimatedPremiumMonthly, estimatedPremiumAnnual, and any validation errors. This state is initialized from getQuoteCoverageOptions and then updated only via responses from updateQuoteCoverageSelections and updateQuoteDiscountEligibility.

The discountSelections structure sent to updateQuoteCoverageSelections and updateQuoteDiscountEligibility is derived from the current discountEligibilityOptions state (for example, an array that pairs each discount code with its current selected/unselected status). No discount configuration is hardcoded; all labels, descriptions, and codes come from getQuoteCoverageOptions.

No localStorage or other persistent client-side storage is used. If the user leaves or reloads the page, the state is reconstructed by re-reading the quoteId from the URL and calling getQuoteCoverageOptions again. All outgoing navigation includes the quote identifier as a URL parameter so that subsequent pages and steps can re-identify the active quote.

components.functionality: Central controller that initializes the page using getQuoteCoverageOptions, maintains the in-memory representation of the current quote’s coverage and discount selections, coordinates calls to updateQuoteCoverageSelections and updateQuoteDiscountEligibility, and drives navigation to other steps.

Parent form that groups policy type, liability, deductible, and discount selection components, ensures that required selections are present, and triggers the final save-and-continue action to move to the plans list.

Displays the currently selected policy type and, if multiple policy types are available for the quote, allows the user to switch between them. Each change updates the quote via updateQuoteCoverageSelections and refreshes estimated premiums.

Presents the available liabilityLimitOptions and deductibleOptions returned by getQuoteCoverageOptions, highlights any recommended options, and lets users choose one liability limit and one deductible. Selections are persisted and priced by calling updateQuoteCoverageSelections.

Lists discountEligibilityOptions for the quote and allows users to toggle eligibility for each. Builds the discountSelections payload and calls updateQuoteDiscountEligibility after each change to apply discounts and update the estimated premium.
Shows the current estimatedPremiumMonthly and estimatedPremiumAnnual for the quote, updating whenever updateQuoteCoverageSelections or updateQuoteDiscountEligibility returns new pricing.

Primary call-to-action within the coverage-options-form. When clicked, validates user selections and triggers updateQuoteCoverageSelections. On a successful response, it redirects to quote_plans.html with URL parameters quoteId (from quoteNumber) and sortBy=price_asc.
Navigates the user back to the boat details step for the current quote (quote_boat_details.html) using the quoteNumber from the controller state as the quoteId URL parameter.

Displays error messages returned from getQuoteCoverageOptions, updateQuoteCoverageSelections, or updateQuoteDiscountEligibility, including field-specific messages. Optionally allows users to dismiss messages after reviewing them.

layout.grid_system: Likely a 12-column fixed-width grid (approx. 1140–1200px) centered within the viewport, with the left 7–8 columns for main content and right 4–5 columns for the sidebar CTA/agent column.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a two-column layout beneath the global red top navigation. Directly under the nav, a slim full-width utility row holds the back navigation link (left) and the quote coverage controller (to its right). Below this row, the layout splits into a left main content column (about 60–65% width) containing the coverage form and a right sidebar column (about 35–40% width) containing the premium summary card with the primary 'View Plans' CTA. Within each column, content flows linearly from top to bottom with clearly separated blocks: the left stacks the error summary (when present) above the coverage form sections; the right stacks the premium summary at the top of the sidebar.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a two-column layout beneath the global red top navigation. Directly under the nav, a slim full-width utility row holds the back navigation link (left) and the quote coverage controller (to its right). Below this row, the layout splits into a left main content column (about 60–65% width) containing the coverage form and a right sidebar column (about 35–40% width) containing the premium summary card with the primary 'View Plans' CTA. Within each column, content flows linearly from top to bottom with clearly separated blocks: the left stacks the error summary (when present) above the coverage form sections; the right stacks the premium summary at the top of the sidebar.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The central content is constrained to a fixed-width grid (approx. 1140–1200px) centered in the viewport. The left column spans roughly 7–8 of 12 grid columns for form content, and the right column spans 4–5 columns for the premium summary. Vertical spacing between major blocks (utility row, form sections, sidebar cards) follows 24–32px gaps, with 20–24px internal padding on cards and 12–16px vertical / 24–32px horizontal padding for pill-shaped CTAs. The premium summary card occupies roughly the upper 40% of the sidebar above the fold so its CTA is immediately visible.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is medium-low: form controls and summary details are information-rich but set in generous whitespace. Each form section has its own breathing room and clear labeling, separated by 24–32px vertical gaps. The premium summary card is concise, showing only the most important pricing and coverage highlights with a prominent CTA, avoiding clutter. The error summary, when visible, appears as a single consolidated block instead of inline errors everywhere, maintaining a clean, corporate look.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye moves in an F-shaped pattern: from the top navigation, down to the utility row (back link and coverage controller), then across to the left column header and first form section. From there, attention is drawn across to the right by the strong red 'View Plans' button within the premium summary card near the top of the sidebar. Users then scan down through the form sections on the left while periodically referencing the premium summary on the right, which remains visible (sticky) to keep the primary CTA and estimated premium anchored in view. The error summary, when present, briefly interrupts this flow by appearing directly above the form, highlighted in red accents.

layout.overall_description: Below the fixed red top navigation, a slim, light-gray utility strip spans the full content width, containing a subtle text back link on the far left and the quote coverage controller beside it. Beneath this strip, the page splits into two columns within a centered, fixed-width grid: on the left, a spacious main column for the coverage options form; on the right, a slightly narrower sidebar for the premium summary. The left column begins with the page heading and, when necessary, a consolidated error summary card, followed by a vertically stacked sequence of form sections (policy type, liability & deductibles, discount eligibility), each clearly separated and easy to scan. The right sidebar starts at the same vertical height as the left column heading, with a white premium summary card floating on a faint gray background, framed by soft shadows and ample padding. Inside this card, the estimated premium details are presented succinctly, and at the bottom sits a large red pill-shaped 'View Plans' button that commands attention. As users scroll down the left form, the right summary card remains pinned within view on desktop, reinforcing the relationship between their coverage choices and the premium, and keeping the primary action accessible at all times. Overall, the layout feels modern, orderly, and trustworthy, with red accents used sparingly to emphasize brand and key CTAs.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote coverage controller sits within a slim, full-width utility row directly beneath the global top navigation and above the two-column layout. It is horizontally aligned to the left half of this utility strip, starting just to the right of the back-to-boat-details-link, sharing the same vertical center. The controller spans roughly 40–50% of the content width (aligned with the left column grid), leaving comfortable breathing room to its right. It appears as a low-height bar or step indicator (about 40–48px tall overall for the utility strip) with a clean, flat style and subtle red accents to indicate progress. There is minimal vertical padding (8–12px) within the strip, keeping it visually light so it doesn’t compete with the main form or sidebar card below. The left main column (coverage-options-form) and right sidebar (premium-summary-panel) both begin approximately 24–32px below this controller row, aligning their top edges to form a clean visual band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in the normal document flow as part of a full-width horizontal strip. It remains fixed in position relative to the page content (not sticky), so as users scroll down through the form and summary, the controller scrolls off the screen along with the utility strip.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The coverage-options-form occupies the left main column beneath the utility strip and page heading, spanning approximately 60–65% of the content width (aligned to 7–8 columns of a 12-column grid). It begins around 24–32px below the utility row, with its left edge aligning precisely with the back-to-boat-details-link and quote-coverage-controller. Vertically, the form is structured into clear, stacked sections: the policy-type-selector at the top, followed by the liability-deductible-selector, then the discount-eligibility-form at the bottom. Each section spans the full width of this left column and is separated from the next by about 24–32px of vertical whitespace. The entire form area uses generous internal spacing: 16–24px padding around field groups and 12–16px spacing between individual controls, keeping the visual density comfortable. When the error-summary is present, it appears directly above the first form section, pushing the form down while maintaining alignment to the left column. The bottom of the form extends well below the fold, with users scrolling vertically through these sections while the sidebar premium-summary-panel remains visible on the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows normally in the left column, expanding vertically as more controls are added. On narrower viewports (e.g., mobile), this column stacks above the sidebar, allowing the form to take full-width while maintaining the same top-to-bottom section order.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy-type-selector is the first and visually most prominent section within the coverage-options-form. It is positioned at the very top of the left column’s form content, immediately below any error-summary (if present) and the page heading, with a vertical gap of roughly 24px. This section spans the full inner width of the form column and reads like a clear card or grouped field set: a concise section heading at the top, followed by one or more options (e.g., radio cards or tiles) arranged in one or two columns depending on space. Internal padding of about 20–24px on all sides keeps the content comfortable, with 12–16px spacing between each option. A bottom margin of 24–32px separates this selector from the next section (liability-deductible-selector), preserving the medium-low density and clean hierarchy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Contained entirely within the coverage-options-form, it behaves like a block-level section that scales to the width of the left column and grows vertically based on the number of options. It scrolls with the form content and has no sticky or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The liability-deductible-selector sits directly below the policy-type-selector within the left form column, separated by a clear vertical gap of about 24–32px. It extends across the full width of the form column and is slightly taller than the policy-type section due to multiple input groups (e.g., liability limit selectors, deductible dropdowns or sliders). A section heading at the top aligns with the headings in other form sections, creating a consistent left margin. Inside, related controls are grouped horizontally where space permits (e.g., liability limit on the left, deductible on the right), with 16–24px horizontal spacing and 12–16px vertical spacing between rows. This section uses a similar card-like structure with 20–24px internal padding, giving it visual separation while still feeling part of the same form. A margin of 24–32px below this section provides clear separation before the discount-eligibility-form.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a standard block section within the coverage-options-form. It grows vertically as needed to accommodate multiple fields and error states, and scrolls naturally with the left column content. There is no special sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The discount-eligibility-form is the final major section within the coverage-options-form, positioned directly below the liability-deductible-selector with a 24–32px vertical gap. It spans the full width of the left column and typically contains a list of checkboxes, toggles, or short input fields for discount questions. A section heading aligns with the other form section headings, followed by grouped discount items in one column, each separated by 8–12px vertically to avoid visual clutter. The entire section has 20–24px of internal padding, matching the card-like treatment of the other sections. A bottom margin of about 32–40px beneath this form section ensures a comfortable visual endpoint to the left column content before any page-level footer or additional steps, while still aligning vertically with the lower part of the premium-summary-panel on the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Like the other form sections, it is a block-level element inside the coverage-options-form, expanding vertically based on the number of discount items. It scrolls with the rest of the left column content and has no sticky or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The premium-summary-panel is the primary sidebar element, positioned in the right column starting at the same vertical level as the left column’s page heading and top of the form content. It occupies approximately 35–40% of the content width, aligned to the right edge of the main grid. The panel is presented as a white card on a very light gray background, with rounded corners and a subtle, soft shadow that lifts it slightly from the page. Generous internal padding (20–24px on all sides) frames the summary content: a concise heading at the top (e.g., 'Estimated Premium'), the current premium amount in a slightly larger, bold type, and a brief list of key coverage highlights. At the bottom of the panel, separated from the summary details by about 16–24px of vertical space, sits the view-plans-cta button, spanning nearly the full width of the card. The card itself takes up roughly the upper 40% of the sidebar height above the fold, ensuring the heading and CTA are visible without scrolling. A 24–32px gap below the card leaves room for any future secondary content in the sidebar, while keeping this card clearly distinguished as the primary sidebar block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, the premium-summary-panel is sticky within the right column: as the user scrolls through the left-column form, the panel remains pinned with a comfortable offset (around 24–32px) from the top of the viewport, ensuring the premium details and primary CTA stay visible. On smaller viewports, it reverts to a standard block element that stacks below or above the form content, scrolling naturally.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The view-plans-cta is rendered as a large, pill-shaped red button anchored at the bottom of the premium-summary-panel card. It spans nearly the full inner width of the card, leaving 16–20px of horizontal padding between the button and the card edges. The button has a substantial height, with approximately 12–16px vertical and 24–32px horizontal internal padding, giving it a bold, touch-friendly appearance. It is positioned about 16–24px below the lowest line of premium summary text, visually separated so it reads as the clear next step. The button’s strong red fill and white text mirror the brand styling from the mockup, making it the focal point of the right sidebar. When the user scrolls, the button remains in a consistent position at the bottom of the sticky premium-summary-panel, so it is almost always visible while the user adjusts coverage options.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The button is a fixed element within the boundaries of the premium-summary-panel; it does not float independently. Its position relative to the card content is static, but because the card itself is sticky in the right column on desktop, the button effectively stays visible in the viewport as the user scrolls the form on the left.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The back-to-boat-details-link is a subtle, text-based navigation link placed in the far-left side of the utility strip directly below the top navigation. It sits horizontally aligned so that its left edge matches the left margin of the main content grid, with about 16px of top and bottom padding within the strip to keep it comfortably clickable without appearing heavy. The link may include a small left-pointing arrow icon to reinforce its backward navigation role, but visually it remains lightweight, using regular-weight text and a red or dark gray color. The quote-coverage-controller is positioned just to the right of this link within the same strip, sharing the vertical centerline. This arrangement clearly signals that the link belongs to the quote flow controls while not competing with the primary CTA in the sidebar.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: It is part of the normal document flow within the utility strip below the nav and scrolls off-screen as the user scrolls down. It is not sticky or fixed, maintaining the mockup’s focus on the main form and sidebar CTA.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error-summary appears only when there are validation issues and is positioned within the left column, directly above the coverage-options-form’s first section (policy-type-selector). It spans the full width of the left column, aligned with the form’s left and right edges, creating a clear, unified block of feedback. The component is styled as a low-profile card or banner with a white or very light background, a prominent red accent (such as a left border or icon), and concise error text. There is approximately 16–24px of padding inside the error card to keep the text readable and allow space for an icon if present. A vertical gap of about 16–24px separates the error-summary from the page heading above and another 16–24px gap separates it from the first form section below, ensuring it is clearly distinguishable but not visually overwhelming. When present, it momentarily becomes one of the first elements the eye hits on entering the form area, before dropping down into the form sections themselves.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The error-summary participates in the normal flow of the left column. It appears and disappears dynamically based on validation state, pushing the form content down when visible. It does not have sticky behavior; as the user scrolls down through the form, the error-summary scrolls out of view like any other block.

name: Quote  Plans List
title: Quote Plans
description: Lists available insurance plans for the active boat insurance quote, lets users sort plans, compare key features, and select a plan to continue to the plan summary.

core_features: Load and display all plans associated with the active quote using the getQuotePlans interface, including pricing and key coverage features.
Allow users to control the sort order of plans (for example by price) via a sort control that updates the sortBy URL parameter and refreshes data through getQuotePlans.
Show each plan’s key attributes at a glance (plan name, policy type, liability limit, deductible, key features, and online availability).
Highlight or otherwise identify featured plans based on the isFeatured flag, using this metadata in list ordering or labeling (without hardcoded styling in this specification).
Indicate which plans are available for online selection using the isAvailableOnline flag, and disable or adjust behavior of selection actions for plans that are not available online.
Provide a "Select Plan" action for eligible plans that calls selectPlanForCurrentQuote and then navigates to the plan summary page with the correct quote identifier in the URL.
Provide navigation back to the coverage options page for the same quote, using the quoteNumber returned by getQuotePlans as the outgoing quoteId URL parameter.

Handle empty or error states from getQuotePlans (for example, no plans returned or failure to load) with functional messaging and retry behavior (without static content details in this specification).
user_workflows:
Workflow: Arrive on plans list from coverage options
1. User clicks a continue action on quote_coverage_options.html, which navigates to quote_plans.html with a quoteId URL parameter and optional sortBy parameter.
2. On page initialization, the page controller reads quoteId and sortBy from the URL.
3. The application context associates the active quote using quoteId, then calls getQuotePlans with the current sortBy value.
4. The page displays the quote identifier returned by getQuotePlans.quoteNumber and renders the plans list using getQuotePlans.plans.
5. User reviews plan options, optionally adjusts sort order, and then either selects a plan or navigates back to coverage options.

Workflow: Change sort order to find a preferred plan
1. User opens the sort control (for example, a select input or radio group) that is bound to the current sortBy state.
2. User chooses a new sort option and submits/changes the control.
3. The sort control handler updates the sortBy URL parameter while preserving the current quoteId parameter.
4. The page controller calls getQuotePlans again with the updated sortBy value.
5. The plans list re-renders using the new order, reflecting the updated sortBy value returned from getQuotePlans.

Workflow: Select an online-available plan and proceed to plan summary
1. In the plans list, user reviews plan rows, including name, policy type, liability limit, deductible, pricing, and keyFeatures indicators.
2. For a plan where isAvailableOnline is true, the user clicks the "Select Plan" action on that row.
3. The select action handler calls selectPlanForCurrentQuote with the planId of the chosen plan.
4. If selectPlanForCurrentQuote returns success = true, the handler reads the returned quoteNumber and nextStep.
5. The handler navigates the browser to quote_plan_summary.html with a quoteId URL parameter equal to the returned quoteNumber.
6. The next page (quote_plan_summary.html) uses that quoteId to continue the quote flow with the selected plan already associated.
7. If selectPlanForCurrentQuote returns success = false or an error, the page shows an error message and leaves the user on the plans list, allowing another selection attempt.

Workflow: Handle plans not available online
1. User reviews the plans list and sees some plans where isAvailableOnline is false.
2. For those plans, the plan row displays plan information but the "Select Plan" action is functionally disabled or replaced with non-interactive text (no call to selectPlanForCurrentQuote is made).
3. User may instead choose a plan where isAvailableOnline is true and use the standard selection workflow, or use navigation controls outside this page to seek offline assistance (such external navigation is not defined by this page’s interfaces).

Workflow: Navigate back to coverage options
1. User decides to change coverage selections instead of choosing a plan.
2. User clicks the back-to-coverage control on quote_plans.html.
3. The handler for this control uses the currently loaded getQuotePlans.quoteNumber as the quoteId value for navigation.
4. The browser navigates to quote_coverage_options.html with quoteId set to that quoteNumber.
5. The coverage options page uses that quoteId to reload the existing quote configuration.

Workflow: Resume plans list from account dashboard
1. From account_dashboard.html, user clicks a link to continue a quote, which navigates to quote_plans.html with a quoteId parameter and optional sortBy.
2. The page initialization logic is the same as when coming from coverage options: read URL parameters, associate active quote from quoteId, then call getQuotePlans with sortBy.
3. User proceeds to review, sort, and select a plan using the same interactions as described above.
interactions:
Interaction: Page initialization
- Trigger: Browser loads quote_plans.html.
- Behavior: The page controller reads quoteId and sortBy from the URL query string. The application context uses quoteId to set the active quote. The controller then calls getQuotePlans with the current sortBy value (which may be empty or a default). On successful response, it stores quoteNumber, sortBy, and plans in page state and triggers rendering of header, sort control, and plans list. On error, it updates state to an error mode for display in the UI.

Interaction: Sort control change/submit
- Trigger: User changes the sort selection and confirms the change (for example, by changing a select input or submitting a small form).
- Behavior: The handler updates the internal sortBy state and updates the browser URL to include the new sortBy value while preserving quoteId. It then calls getQuotePlans with the updated sortBy, replacing the existing plans state with the returned plans. If the call fails, the previous list can remain visible with an error indicator, or an empty/error state is shown, depending on implementation policy.

Interaction: Select plan
- Trigger: User clicks the "Select Plan" button on a plan row where isAvailableOnline is true.
- Behavior: The handler reads the planId from the clicked row and calls selectPlanForCurrentQuote with that planId. While awaiting a response, the row or page can show a loading state (functionally, user input on plan selection is temporarily disabled to prevent duplicate submissions). On success, the handler uses the returned quoteNumber to build the URL for quote_plan_summary.html, setting the quoteId query parameter equal to quoteNumber, then navigates to that URL. On failure, the handler updates error state for that interaction and keeps the user on the current page with the list intact.

Interaction: Attempt to select a non-online plan
- Trigger: User attempts to interact with the select control on a plan row where isAvailableOnline is false.
- Behavior: Either no click handler is bound (control is disabled), or the handler prevents calling selectPlanForCurrentQuote and instead may set or display a non-blocking informational message. No navigation and no backend interface calls occur.

Interaction: Back to coverage options
- Trigger: User clicks a back-navigation control labeled for returning to coverage options.
- Behavior: The handler reads the current quoteNumber from the latest getQuotePlans response. It builds a navigation URL for quote_coverage_options.html with quoteId set to that quoteNumber, then navigates to that URL. No call to selectPlanForCurrentQuote is made in this interaction.

Interaction: Toggle price display unit (if implemented)
- Trigger: User toggles a control that switches how prices are displayed (for example, between monthly and annual views).
- Behavior: The handler updates a local display state (for example, selected billing frequency). It does not call any backend interface; instead, it chooses whether to show totalPriceMonthly or totalPriceAnnual (or the corresponding basePrice values) in each plan row. URL parameters are not changed by this interaction unless the application explicitly wants to persist display preferences via the URL.

Interaction: Expand or collapse key features details (if implemented)
- Trigger: User clicks an expand/collapse control on a plan row to see more or fewer key features.
- Behavior: The handler toggles a local expanded/collapsed state for that plan row only. No backend calls and no URL parameter changes occur; the features are already present in keyFeatures from getQuotePlans.

Interaction: Retry after failed plan load
- Trigger: After a failure from getQuotePlans, user clicks a retry control in an error/empty-state component.
- Behavior: The handler reuses the current quoteId and sortBy values from URL or state, then calls getQuotePlans again with the same sortBy value. On success, normal rendering resumes; on failure, the error state remains.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page’s primary state is driven by URL parameters and the responses of getQuotePlans and selectPlanForCurrentQuote. The URL uses two parameters: quoteId and sortBy. On first load, the page controller reads quoteId and sortBy from the URL. The application context uses quoteId to set or verify the active Quote entity; getQuotePlans does not take quoteId as a parameter and instead operates on the active quote. The sortBy value from the URL is passed directly to getQuotePlans. The response from getQuotePlans defines the current quoteNumber (which is treated as the authoritative external identifier passed onward as quoteId when navigating to other pages) and returns the effective sortBy and the plans array.
